item_description.p: An optical scope that can be added to any rifle. It adds 50% to the rifle's range increment.
Rarity: Civilian
An electronic scope that includes optical sight along with a laster range finder and laser targeting dot that can be added to any rifle. It places the dot on the payload where it will be delivered.

It adds 50% to the rifle's range increment and grants advantage to a roll to hit. But, it can only be used for a single shot per turn and is the only action that can be taken by the user on that turn.
Rarity: Military
Can be applied to any weapon. May or may not require new components to be added to the weapon.
Marksmen recieve +5 bonus to hit at all ranges. Can not be combined with other sights or scopes.
Rarity: Civilian
A flashlight attached, which can be switched on to illuminate targets and eliminate darkness penalties within 100m.
Security: Civilian

A flashlight attached, which can be switched on to illuminate targets and eliminate darkness penalties within 100m. It can be used in either the visible light spectrum or the Infrared. In IR mode only those with infrared vision benefit.
Rarity: Security
This attachment places a red dot where the payload will be delivered. This gives an advantage a roll to hit within 10 m.
Rarity: Security
A silencer that alters a weapon so that it makes very little noise when it fires compared to a normal weapon of its type. Useful for stealth missions.
This device attaches under the barrel of a rifle and is a single-use grapple zipline. has a one-use before needing to be reloaded w/special tools and taking significant time.

This extremely strong 10 m thin cord retracts into a spring-loaded pouch that can clip to a belt. It can be used as a rope to climb up or down or across distances. It has a built-in pulley- based zipline handle, and its end has a very effective magnegrapple, which can be used to grapple most surfaces. It’s a very versatile tool for getting around in the wilderness, but can be equally useful for a burglar or spy.
Rarity: Security

This weapon attaches under the barrel of a rifle and that pneumatically fires a normal grenade amazing distances with a fair amount of accuracy. The pneumatics are actuated by a long pump on the nozzle, which fills an air reservoir concealed in the rifle’s stock with compacted air pressure. Pulling the trigger releases the grenade with an identifiable “whoomph” noise.
Rarity: Military

Requires that a weapon be reworked to have the grip and trigger mechanism replaced with thie recognition grip. Once fitted, the weapon will only fire for those it’s programmed to recognize. It takes time to recalibrate it to a new user.
Increases the capacity of a bullet or gyrojet magazine by 50%.
Weapons with an extended magazine can not be concealed or quickly drawn from a holster.
Rarity: Security
Their Black series are their pinnacle series. These bring all their technolgies together; longer range, increased damage, and premium sights. At premium prices.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges

item_description.p: Description: This is a rifle-sized version of the gyrojet pistol. It fires the same self-propelled gyrojet mini rockets, but fires them through a longer barrel to give them more velocity. Grooves along the inside of the rifle force a spin on the mini rocket, helping achieve a longer range than the pistol can provide. The magazine holds a payload of 15 gyrojets, and, despite the greater range, the mini rockets cause the same 3D damage as the pistol. If the rumors exist about variant gyrojet ammunition, this is by far a superior method of delivery.
Designed to be a straightforward but high powered version of standard firearms. Marketed for Close Quarters Battle and urban engagements.

Description: When you really want to make an impression on your enemies, walk casually towards them firing off shot after shot with an assault shotgun. It’s loud and powerful, produces a powerful kick, and its scattershot fires in a wide enough area that you don’t have to be too accurate to paint a target with pain. A pump- action chambers the first round, but thereafter it shoots one scattershot from its 10-shot box magazine payload every time the trigger is pulled, causing 3D damage to targets within its listed range increment. Like all scattershot, it loses effectiveness with range. Each range increment beyond the first reduces the damage by 1D, until it’s out of damage dice and then just causes cosmetic damage and annoys people.
Designed to be a straightforward but high powered version of standard firearms. Marketed for Close Quarters Battle and urban engagements.

Description: A fairly common military weapon (and favored among bodyguards and thugs), the auto pistol is an automatic sidearm that fires a standard bullet type from a boxed payload magazine. The weapon can fire in one of two modes: semi- automatic and burstfire. In semiautomatic mode, it fires one bullet every time it’s used, which causes 2D damage. In burstfire mode, it fires a burst of 5 bullets as one attack, offering +20 to hit and causing 5D damage. Bullets are interchangeable with all “bullet” weapons and partial magazines can be turned into full payloads by emptying other partial magazines of other bullet-firing weapons.
Designed to be a straightforward but high powered version of standard firearms. Marketed for Close Quarters Battle and urban engagements.

Description: This lightweight elegant weapon (often called a “blaster rifle”) is the laser equivalent of the auto rifle and a larger version of the laser pistol. It fires a longer blue beam than the laser pistol and is popular among elite soldiers and Special Forces. The longer lasing chamber allows for a more collimated beam and greater range. In semiautomatic mode, the laser rifle can be set to use 1 EU to 5 EU per shot, doing 1D(B) damage per EU spent. In burstfire mode, the wielder receives a +20 bonus to hit and the weapon fires a burst of ten small 1 EU shots. The burst is fired as one action, with one attack roll. If successful, enough of the beams hit their targets to cause 6D(B) damage
The White Series is designed for longer range battlefields with superior barrel quality and sights. Marketed for wilderness and regional operations.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges

item_description.p: Description: This is a heavier rifle-sized version of the auto pistol. It fires the same ammunition (it’s interchangeable), but due to the longer barrel it has a superior range and generates more speed upon discharge. The box magazine holds more bullets as well. Like the auto pistol, this rifle can be set to semiautomatic or burstfire mode. In semiautomatic mode, it fires one bullet every time it’s used, which causes 3D damage. In burstfire mode, it fires a burst of 5 bullets as one attack, offering +20 to hit and causing 6D damage. This weapon is very durable and is used by most armies throughout the galaxy due to the inexpensiveness of the ammunition.
The White Series is designed for longer range battlefields with superior barrel quality and sights. Marketed for wilderness and regional operations.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges

item_description.p: Description: This long rifle has a series of coils along its length that carry an electric charge, building up size and momentum in a familiar “crackle-boom” as a ball of lightning flies towards its target. This weapon is similar in effectiveness to the disruptor rifle, but its ball of lightning simply burns and shocks the cells of the body (causing damage, but not the agonizing pain of a disruptor). Each shot drains 4 EU from the energy source and causes 4D(E) damage. Despite the large drain on its source, this weapon is quite popular due to its high stopping power (and the fact that few people have protection against electrical damage).
The White Series is designed for longer range battlefields with superior barrel quality and sights. Marketed for wilderness and regional operations.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges

item_description.p: Description: Often called a “blaster,” this hand- held pistol is capable of firing an intense beam of charged energy. The beam is a nearly instantaneous blue pulse, and it makes a very distinctive sound. Although expensive, the laser pistol is a favorite throughout the galaxy. It requires a standard energy source and has a power setting, allowing it to fire anywhere from 1 to 5 EU per shot, causing 1D(B) per EU spent on the shot. It is a modern and elegant weapon, shows you are a person of status and severity, and is worth the money.
The White Series is designed for longer range battlefields with superior barrel quality and sights. Marketed for wilderness and regional operations.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges

item_description.p: Description: The sniper rifle is the long range version of the semiauto rifle. Its improved design provides three times the range of the auto and semiauto rifles. It has a 20-round standard bullet magazine and only fires in semiautomatic mode. A successful hit with the sniper rifle causes only 3D damage, but every turn spent aiming the sniper rifle at an unaware target gives a +10 to hit and a +1D to damage, up to a maximum of +30 and +3D.
There is no doubt who the Grey Series of firearms is designed for. These firearms are highly sought by bodyguards and covert operatives alike.

list.li: Laser Target Red Dot, advantage on rolls to hit within 10m
Compact, +20% to CRD check to draw as one action

item_description.p: Description: A fairly common military weapon (and favored among bodyguards and thugs), the auto pistol is an automatic sidearm that fires a standard bullet type from a boxed payload magazine. The weapon can fire in one of two modes: semi- automatic and burstfire. In semiautomatic mode, it fires one bullet every time it’s used, which causes 2D damage. In burstfire mode, it fires a burst of 5 bullets as one attack, offering +20 to hit and causing 5D damage. Bullets are interchangeable with all “bullet” weapons and partial magazines can be turned into full payloads by emptying other partial magazines of other bullet-firing weapons.
There is no doubt who the Grey Series of firearms is designed for. These firearms are highly sought by bodyguards and covert operatives alike.

list.li: Laser Target Red Dot, advantage on rolls to hit within 10m
Compact, +20% to CRD check to draw as one action

item_description.p: Description: These heavy pistols fire tiny self- propelled rockets that build up speed, fly through the air in a straight shot, and strike a target. The payload magazine holds ten such gyrojet rockets. Each has a decent range and can cause 3D damage. Despite the small payload of ammunition, this weapon is popular because it lacks recoil and packs a penetrating punch. Rumors exist of variant gyrojet ammunition types that explode on impact rather than simply penetrate – but those rumors are not confirmed. Like all gyrojet weapons, it takes some moments for the gyrojet rockets to accelerate up to impact speed, so they’re harmless at 3 meters or less.
There is no doubt who the Grey Series of firearms is designed for. These firearms are highly sought by bodyguards and covert operatives alike.

list.li: Laser Target Red Dot, advantage on rolls to hit within 10m
Compact, +20% to CRD check to draw as one action

item_description.p: Description: Often called a “blaster,” this hand- held pistol is capable of firing an intense beam of charged energy. The beam is a nearly instantaneous blue pulse, and it makes a very distinctive sound. Although expensive, the laser pistol is a favorite throughout the galaxy. It requires a standard energy source and has a power setting, allowing it to fire anywhere from 1 to 5 EU per shot, causing 1D(B) per EU spent on the shot. It is a modern and elegant weapon, shows you are a person of status and severity, and is worth the money.

Description: This pistol has rows of coils along its length that generate a cascading electric arc to flash and burn its way through the air like a ball of lightning. It makes a zapping, crackling noise as it is fired, and for this reason it is often nicknamed a “zapgun.” Each shot drains 2 EU from its energy source. It has a relatively short range, but is versatile and popular among law enforcers and bounty hunters due to its two settings: if set to “harm” the weapon causes 2D(E) damage per shot; if set to “Stun,” it can cause unconsciousness or temporarily stun enemies (described in Referee’s Handbook).
Their Black series are their pinnacle series. These bring all their technolgies together; longer range, increased damage, and premium sights. At premium prices.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges

It’s loud and powerful, produces a powerful kick, and its scattershot fires in a wide enough area that you don’t have to be too accurate to paint a target with pain. A pump- action chambers the first round, but thereafter it shoots one scattershot from its 10-shot box magazine payload every time the trigger is pulled, causing 3D damage to targets within its listed range increment. Like all scattershot, it loses effectiveness with range. Each range increment beyond the first reduces the damage by 1D, until it’s out of damage dice and then just causes cosmetic damage and annoys people.
Their Black series are their pinnacle series. These bring all their technolgies together; longer range, increased damage, and premium sights. At premium prices.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges

item_description.p: Description: A fairly common military weapon (and favored among bodyguards and thugs), the auto pistol is an automatic sidearm that fires a standard bullet type from a boxed payload magazine. The weapon can fire in one of two modes: semi- automatic and burstfire. In semiautomatic mode, it fires one bullet every time it’s used, which causes 2D damage. In burstfire mode, it fires a burst of 5 bullets as one attack, offering +20 to hit and causing 5D damage. Bullets are interchangeable with all “bullet” weapons and partial magazines can be turned into full payloads by emptying other partial magazines of other bullet-firing weapons.
Their Black series are their pinnacle series. These bring all their technolgies together; longer range, increased damage, and premium sights. At premium prices.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges

item_description.p: Description: This rifle is outlawed on most worlds. It is designed to disrupt and destabilize the target’s nervous system through tremendous sonic agitation that causes tissue trauma and intense pain. There is no stun setting; the designers of this weapon didn’t want to capture someone with it. The weapon simply draws 4 EU from its energy source per shot and fires a nearly invisible silvery wave of sonic energy, causing 4D(S) damage to its targets. The feeling has been described as if every nerve in the body were being pulled and snipped simultaneously. It’s a cruel, sadistic weapon.
Their Black series are their pinnacle series. These bring all their technolgies together; longer range, increased damage, and premium sights. At premium prices.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges
Grenade Cylinder, holds 6 grenades, takes 2 turns to reload, ordered is detemrined when loaded

item_description.p: Description: This is a rifle-sized object that pneumatically fires a normal grenade amazing distances with a fair amount of accuracy. The pneumatics are actuated by a long pump on the nozzle, which fills an air reservoir concealed in the rifle’s stock with compacted air pressure. Pulling the trigger releases the grenade with an identifiable “whoomph” noise. Older forms of grenade rifles used special grenade-like bullets that had to be purchased separately, but modern grenade rifles are marvels of pneumatic payload delivery.
Their Black series are their pinnacle series. These bring all their technolgies together; longer range, increased damage, and premium sights. At premium prices.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges

item_description.p: Description: This lightweight elegant weapon (often called a “blaster rifle”) is the laser equivalent of the auto rifle and a larger version of the laser pistol. It fires a longer blue beam than the laser pistol and is popular among elite soldiers and Special Forces. The longer lasing chamber allows for a more collimated beam and greater range. In semiautomatic mode, the laser rifle can be set to use 1 EU to 5 EU per shot, doing 1D(B) damage per EU spent. In burstfire mode, the wielder receives a +20 bonus to hit and the weapon fires a burst of ten small 1 EU shots. The burst is fired as one action, with one attack roll. If successful, enough of the beams hit their targets to cause 6D(B) damage
Their Black series are their pinnacle series. These bring all their technolgies together; longer range, increased damage, and premium sights. At premium prices.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges

item_description.p: Description: The sniper laser rifle (“sniper blaster”) is a high-end laser weapon. It has an extremely long lasing cavity, giving superior collimation for the laser beam and resulting in a ten times greater range than the laser rifle. It’s the longest-range non-heavy weapon in the galaxy. The sniper laser rifle attaches to a standard energy source and drains 5 EU per shot, delivering 5D(B) damage. It has no burst mode. In addition, due to the improved nature of the sighting mechanism, every turn spent aiming the sniper rifle adds +10 to hit and +1D(B) to the damage up to a maximum of +30 to hit and +3D(B) damage. You want someone with this weapon stationed somewhere in the hills nearby. But only if he’s an ally.

These are a type of Advanced Gyrojet round. Orginally invented by Lampyridae Munitions company they are now manufactured by several companies but all have the same (nominal) capabilities. They are generally considered illegal for private ownership/use. Government forces and some mercenary companies are licensed to purchase and posses them.
These rounds add the following:

list.li: Armor Piercing rounds, i.e. penetrating rounds half damage to suits, and half to target. Screens protect fully.

item_description.p: Rarity: Military

Description: The mini rockets of gyrojet-based weaponry come in grouped clips that speed-load into a slot in the weapon. They don’t come in boxed magazines, and can be somewhat bulky to carry. You can’t easily conceal them, like you can a bullet-based boxed magazine. Interestingly enough, gyrojet rifles can use 10-gyrojet or 15- gyrojet clips interchangeably, though the pistols can only use the 10-gyrojet configurations.
Individual mini rockets can be removed from the clips to consolidate many partially depleted clips into fewer fuller ones. 3-gyrojet clips are for wristrockets.

These are a type of Advanced Gyrojet round. Invented and manufactured by Lampyridae Munitions company. The production of them is currently limited and are only sold in limited locations, quantities and to "approved Lampyridae partners".
These rounds add the following:

list.li: Damage from these rounds are all Electric Type i.e. 3D(E)

item_description.p: Rarity: Cutting Edge

Description: The mini rockets of gyrojet-based weaponry come in grouped clips that speed-load into a slot in the weapon. They don’t come in boxed magazines, and can be somewhat bulky to carry. You can’t easily conceal them, like you can a bullet-based boxed magazine. Interestingly enough, gyrojet rifles can use 10-gyrojet or 15- gyrojet clips interchangeably, though the pistols can only use the 10-gyrojet configurations.
Individual mini rockets can be removed from the clips to consolidate many partially depleted clips into fewer fuller ones. 3-gyrojet clips are for wristrockets.
These are a type of Advanced Gyrojet round. Orginally invented by Lampyridae Munitions company they are now manufactured by several companies but all have the same (nominal) capabilities.
These rounds add the following:

list.li: Extended Range rounds +20m range, but only do 2D damage,

item_description.p: Rarity: Security

These rounds add the following:

list.li: Damage from these rounds are all Sonic Type i.e. 3D(S)

item_description.p: Rarity: Cutting Edge

Designed and manufactured by Lampyridae Munitions. This weapon has the following traits.

list.li: Rugged designs that are known to rarely misfire even in extreme circumstances, but they are bulky. -20% to CRD checks to quickdraw the weapon as a single action.
Add 50% to the number of shots in one payload. If weapon uses EU, reduce the EU drain per shot by 1 (minimum 1 EU/shot).

It fires the same self-propelled gyrojet mini rockets, but fires them through a longer barrel to give them more velocity. Grooves along the inside of the rifle force a spin on the mini rocket, helping achieve a longer range than the pistol can provide. list.li: Rugged designs that are known to rarely misfire even in extreme circumstances, but they are bulky. -20% to CRD checks to quickdraw the weapon as a single action.

item_description.p: Description: These heavy pistols fire tiny self- propelled rockets that build up speed, fly through the air in a straight shot, and strike a target. The payload magazine holds ten such gyrojet rockets. Each has a decent range and can cause 3D damage. Despite the small payload of ammunition, this weapon is popular because it lacks recoil and packs a penetrating punch. Rumors exist of variant gyrojet ammunition types that explode on impact rather than simply penetrate – but those rumors are not confirmed. Like all gyrojet weapons, it takes some moments for the gyrojet rockets to accelerate up to impact speed, so they’re harmless at 3 meters or less.
Using cutting edge technology, these sonic weapons are silent and just as dangerous as their competitors.

list.li: Silent. Weapon makes virtually nonoise when it fires

item_description.p: Rarity: Security

Description: This pistol is an energy weapon capable of stunning an opponent by specific application of ultrasonic energy to destabilize the victim’s nervous system. The weapon’s beam makes a distinctive buzzing noise and a shimmering wave effect, but otherwise is invisible. It has a standard slot for an energy source and has two settings: “normal” and “high.” When set to “normal,” it drains 1 EU per shot and causes a Stun (S) effect. When set to “high” mode, it drains 5 EU per shot, and anyone hit by the beam has a -20 to his STR resistance check against the Stun(S) effect. Because it is non-lethal, it is one of the only ranged weapon types permitted for self-defense on some worlds.
Using cutting edge technology, these sonic weapons are silent and just as dangerous as their competitors.

list.li: Silent. Weapon makes virtually nonoise when it fires

item_description.p: Rarity: Military

Description: This rifle is outlawed on most worlds. It is designed to disrupt and destabilize the target’s nervous system through tremendous sonic agitation that causes tissue trauma and intense pain. There is no stun setting; the designers of this weapon didn’t want to capture someone with it. The weapon simply draws 4 EU from its energy source per shot and fires a nearly invisible silvery wave of sonic energy, causing 4D(S) damage to its targets. The feeling has been described as if every nerve in the body were being pulled and snipped simultaneously. It’s a cruel, sadistic weapon.
Using cutting edge technology, these sonic weapons are silent and just as dangerous as their competitors.

list.li: Silent. Weapon makes virtually nonoise when it fires

item_description.p: Rarity: Military

Description: This heavy sonic weapon is shaped like a horseshoe, creating its sonic wave between the two heavy probes and hurling it in the direction of the enemy. Unlike most sonic attacks, the silver wave is far from invisible and is also far from silent. It creates long thrumming noises as it builds up its damage effect then releases the wave in a powerful blast of bright silver energy. The devastator drains 10 EU per shot from an attached energy source and causes 1Dx10(S) damage. Devastator damage is too quick and violent to cause the agonizing results of a Disruptor and is not generally outlawed.
This line of sonic weapons have an improve lithium system that reduces power consumption.

list.li: Silent. Weapon makes virtually nonoise when it fires
Improved Power Consumption, power drain reduced by 1 EU

item_description.p: Rarity: Security

list.li: Silent. Weapon makes virtually nonoise when it fires
Improved Power Consumption, power drain reduced by 2 EU

item_description.p: Rarity: Cutting Edge

Description: This heavy sonic weapon is shaped like a horseshoe, creating its sonic wave between the two heavy probes and hurling it in the direction of the enemy. Unlike most sonic attacks, the silver wave is far from invisible and is also far from silent. It creates long thrumming noises as it builds up its damage effect then releases the wave in a powerful blast of bright silver energy. The devastator drains 10 EU per shot from an attached energy source and causes 1Dx10(S) damage. Devastator damage is too quick and violent to cause the agonizing results of a Disruptor and is not generally outlawed.
A line of elite combat-rated suits for special forces. Not much more than prototypes in field trials by select unit of the Federation Task Force.

list.li: Ablative Ballistic Plates (ABP), +20 BP protection, not repairable

item_description.p: Description: These are white or chrome suits of special material designed to deflect or refract laser beams. They provide extra Body Points that take beam damage until reduced to 0 BP(B). In many ways they work just like ballistic suits but against beam weapons instead of physical weapons. Reflec suits can be repaired by a technician if 1 or more BP(B) remain. Civilian reflec suits can pass for common clothes and provide 10 BP(B). Security reflec suits provide 25 BP(B) and have a shiny glean that cannot be mistaken for casual clothes (but can be hidden beneath bulky clothes or heavy long coats). Military reflec suits provide 50 BP(B) and are very obvious; the character is clearly wearing a shiny chrome or white body suit.
A line of elite combat-rated suits for special forces. Not much more than prototypes in field trials by select unit of the Federation Task Force.

list.li: Ablative Ballistic Plates (ABP), +20 BP protection, not repairable

item_description.p: Description: These provide extra Body Points to protect the wearer from physical damage. They do not protect against beam, electric, or sonic damage, but will protect against the most common types of damage, such as from bullets, swords, and the claws of beasts. Once the suit is reduced to 0 BP, any additional damage applies to the character and the suit is trashed. A ballistic suit can be repaired by a technician with a techkit, but only if it has 1 or more BP left.

Civilian suits provide only 10 BP but are totally indistinguishable from normal clothes. Security suits provide 25 BP and appear as light body armor or paramilitary or security uniforms, though they can be concealed beneath long jackets or bulky clothes. Military suits offer 50 BP but cannot be hidden; it’s obvious the character is wearing body armor.
This line of defensive suits are favored by covert operatives. Though hard to acquire, these are the product that put Stalwart on the defense industry map.

list.li: +20 to resistance checks Stun (E) and Stun(S) checks

item_description.p: Description: These are white or chrome suits of special material designed to deflect or refract laser beams. They provide extra Body Points that take beam damage until reduced to 0 BP(B). In many ways they work just like ballistic suits but against beam weapons instead of physical weapons. Reflec suits can be repaired by a technician if 1 or more BP(B) remain. Civilian reflec suits can pass for common clothes and provide 10 BP(B). Security reflec suits provide 25 BP(B) and have a shiny glean that cannot be mistaken for casual clothes (but can be hidden beneath bulky clothes or heavy long coats). Military reflec suits provide 50 BP(B) and are very obvious; the character is clearly wearing a shiny chrome or white body suit.
This line of defensive suits are favored by covert operatives. Though hard to acquire, these are the product that put Stalwart on the defense industry map.

list.li: +20 to resistance checks Stun (E) and Stun(S) checks

item_description.p: Description: These provide extra Body Points to protect the wearer from physical damage. They do not protect against beam, electric, or sonic damage, but will protect against the most common types of damage, such as from bullets, swords, and the claws of beasts. Once the suit is reduced to 0 BP, any additional damage applies to the character and the suit is trashed. A ballistic suit can be repaired by a technician with a techkit, but only if it has 1 or more BP left.

Civilian suits provide only 10 BP but are totally indistinguishable from normal clothes. Security suits provide 25 BP and appear as light body armor or paramilitary or security uniforms, though they can be concealed beneath long jackets or bulky clothes. Military suits offer 50 BP but cannot be hidden; it’s obvious the character is wearing body armor.
This is their budget friendly no frills line. They work just fine, but if you are putting your life on the line, probably not what you want, but what you will find almost everywhere.

list.li: Cheap Construction, if fired more than once per turn, any failed roll to hit jams the weapon.

item_description.p: Description: When you really want to make an impression on your enemies, walk casually towards them firing off shot after shot with an assault shotgun. It’s loud and powerful, produces a powerful kick, and its scattershot fires in a wide enough area that you don’t have to be too accurate to paint a target with pain. A pump- action chambers the first round, but thereafter it shoots one scattershot from its 10-shot box magazine payload every time the trigger is pulled, causing 3D damage to targets within its listed range increment. Like all scattershot, it loses effectiveness with range. Each range increment beyond the first reduces the damage by 1D, until it’s out of damage dice and then just causes cosmetic damage and annoys people.
This is their budget friendly no frills line. They work just fine, but if you are putting your life on the line, probably not what you want, but what you will find almost everywhere.

list.li: Cheap Construction, if fired more than once per turn, any failed roll to hit jams the weapon.

item_description.p: Description: A fairly common military weapon (and favored among bodyguards and thugs), the auto pistol is an automatic sidearm that fires a standard bullet type from a boxed payload magazine. The weapon can fire in one of two modes: semi- automatic and burstfire. In semiautomatic mode, it fires one bullet every time it’s used, which causes 2D damage. In burstfire mode, it fires a burst of 5 bullets as one attack, offering +20 to hit and causing 5D damage. Bullets are interchangeable with all “bullet” weapons and partial magazines can be turned into full payloads by emptying other partial magazines of other bullet-firing weapons.
This is their budget friendly no frills line. They work just fine, but if you are putting your life on the line, probably not what you want, but what you will find almost everywhere.

list.li: Cheap Construction, if fired more than once per turn, any failed roll to hit jams the weapon.

item_description.p: Description: This is a heavier rifle-sized version of the auto pistol. It fires the same ammunition (it’s interchangeable), but due to the longer barrel it has a superior range and generates more speed upon discharge. The box magazine holds more bullets as well. Like the auto pistol, this rifle can be set to semiautomatic or burstfire mode. In semiautomatic mode, it fires one bullet every time it’s used, which causes 3D damage. In burstfire mode, it fires a burst of 5 bullets as one attack, offering +20 to hit and causing 6D damage. This weapon is very durable and is used by most armies throughout the galaxy due to the inexpensiveness of the ammunition.
This is their budget friendly no frills line. They work just fine, but if you are putting your life on the line, probably not what you want, but what you will find almost everywhere.

list.li: Cheap Construction, if fired more than once per turn, any failed roll to hit jams the weapon.

item_description.p: Description: Not everyone can afford to shell out hundreds of credits for a laser pistol, but many want the advantages of modern beam weaponry. For these people, the slightly lower-tech plasma- based blastpistol is just the right tool for the job. It is a little bulky, but it does pack quite a punch. When fired, the energy from its EU source is focused through inexpensive crystal membranes and releases in a rather large, nasty red blast that sounds thunderous rather than elegant. It’s followed by a high-pitched whine while the crystal membranes perform a quick regeneration. It is not a stealthy pistol. Each shot drains 2 EU from its energy source and causes 2D(B) damage.
This is their budget friendly no frills line. They work just fine, but if you are putting your life on the line, probably not what you want, but what you will find almost everywhere.

list.li: Cheap Construction, if fired more than once per turn, any failed roll to hit jams the weapon.

item_description.p: Description: These heavy pistols fire tiny self- propelled rockets that build up speed, fly through the air in a straight shot, and strike a target. The payload magazine holds ten such gyrojet rockets. Each has a decent range and can cause 3D damage. Despite the small payload of ammunition, this weapon is popular because it lacks recoil and packs a penetrating punch. Rumors exist of variant gyrojet ammunition types that explode on impact rather than simply penetrate – but those rumors are not confirmed. Like all gyrojet weapons, it takes some moments for the gyrojet rockets to accelerate up to impact speed, so they’re harmless at 3 meters or less.
This is their budget friendly no frills line. They work just fine, but if you are putting your life on the line, probably not what you want, but what you will find almost everywhere.

list.li: Cheap Construction, if fired more than once per turn, any failed roll to hit jams the weapon.

item_description.p: Description: This is a rifle-sized version of the gyrojet pistol. It fires the same self-propelled gyrojet mini rockets, but fires them through a longer barrel to give them more velocity. Grooves along the inside of the rifle force a spin on the mini rocket, helping achieve a longer range than the pistol can provide. The magazine holds a payload of 15 gyrojets, and, despite the greater range, the mini rockets cause the same 3D damage as the pistol. If the rumors exist about variant gyrojet ammunition, this is by far a superior method of delivery.
This is their budget friendly no frills line. They work just fine, but if you are putting your life on the line, probably not what you want, but what you will find almost everywhere.

list.li: Cheap Construction, if fired more than once per turn, any failed roll to hit jams the weapon.

item_description.p: Description: Often called a “blaster,” this hand- held pistol is capable of firing an intense beam of charged energy. The beam is a nearly instantaneous blue pulse, and it makes a very distinctive sound. Although expensive, the laser pistol is a favorite throughout the galaxy. It requires a standard energy source and has a power setting, allowing it to fire anywhere from 1 to 5 EU per shot, causing 1D(B) per EU spent on the shot. It is a modern and elegant weapon, shows you are a person of status and severity, and is worth the money.
This is their budget friendly no frills line. They work just fine, but if you are putting your life on the line, probably not what you want, but what you will find almost everywhere.

list.li: Cheap Construction, if fired more than once per turn, any failed roll to hit jams the weapon.

item_description.p: Description: This lightweight elegant weapon (often called a “blaster rifle”) is the laser equivalent of the auto rifle and a larger version of the laser pistol. It fires a longer blue beam than the laser pistol and is popular among elite soldiers and Special Forces. The longer lasing chamber allows for a more collimated beam and greater range. In semiautomatic mode, the laser rifle can be set to use 1 EU to 5 EU per shot, doing 1D(B) damage per EU spent. In burstfire mode, the wielder receives a +20 bonus to hit and the weapon fires a burst of ten small 1 EU shots. The burst is fired as one action, with one attack roll. If successful, enough of the beams hit their targets to cause 6D(B) damage

notes.p: This automated defensive system is usually connected to a facility security system and computer, but can operate in standalone mode.

This 6060 version is configured with a Scanner score of 60% and a Targeting system of 60% When connected to a security computer, the security computer score is used, in Standalone mode the computer score is 40%

This system uses a modified infrared scanner that has a passive IR range of 50m and an active range of 500m. Detection and identification of targets is a skill check of Computer + Scanner score (80% in standalone mode). The system is usually in passive mode, but will go to active scans as directed by a linked security system, which uses 1 EU / turn.
This system is configured with an Electric Rifle in it's full range turret (360 horizontal and 180 degree elevation).The rifle has a 15m Range increment and uses 4EU per shot.
The ####AR systems are armored with a reflective mesh (25 BP(B) worth) and strengthed to better resist damage.
The system has an attached EU backpack (usually hidden in a nearby wall or cealing) to power the rifle and active scans.
This automated defensive system is usually connected to a facility security system and computer, but can operate in standalone mode.

This 4040 version is configured with a Scanner score of 40% and a Targeting system of 40% When connected to a security computer, the security computer score is used, in Standalone mode the computer score is 20%

This system uses a modified infrared scanner that has a passive IR range of 50m and an active range of 500m. Detection and identification of targets is a skill check of Computer + Scanner score (60% in standalone mode). The system is usually in passive mode, but will go to active scans as directed by a linked security system, which uses 1 EU / turn.
This system is configured with an Electric Rifle in it's full range turret (360 horizontal and 180 degree elevation).The rifle has a 15m Range increment and uses 4EU per shot.
The system has an attached EU backpack (usually hidden in a nearby wall or cealing) to power the rifle and active scans.

list.li: Some type of rifle is typical as a primary weapon
Some type of pistol sidearm as a backup

item_description.p: Comms: Audio, text

Description: This is a device worn on the wrist. It functions as a very modern digital watch and includes an RF radio with a range of 5 km. It’s audio only, but that is normally sufficient. On a civilized world, it will connect to digital communication networks and allow unlimited range. Wristcoms are nearly as popular as hand- held communicators, and are powered by the same microbatteries designed to last the lifetime of ownership. If the Referee permits, wristcoms can come in other forms which allow hands-free audio-only communication, such as earpieces with boom-mics or badges worn on the chest with an included insignia.

Description: This is basically a hand-held video recorder, able to take video and audio recordings in a narrow or wide-field mode. When used passively, it simply records all that is seen or heard. When used actively by draining 2 EU from the attached energy source, it takes an extraordinarily detailed holographic full-range snapshot/image, which it can store and send to a computer for more detailed 3-dimensional analysis.

Description: The passive use of this hand-held device gives voice to the hand that holds it. The handle reads the bioelectric field generated by the user and, with practice, can turn thought into audio. This is used by mutes in the galaxy to allow them a voice where they have none of their own. It’s also common to integrate with artificial intelligences, such as robots, to give them the ability to speak. Vocal scanners are programmed with many languages and can be activated (drains 2 EU) to enable translator circuits to allow a character’s thoughts to be spoken in another galactic standard language, though it won’t help a character understand what is spoken back.
Connections: 4

Description: These are large but flat backpacks, hardened and sturdy. They’re more commonly used by wealthy explorers or laser-equipped military units. They have four retractable cables allowing them to connect to up to four devices and provide 100 EU of energy. EU devices are not rechargeable and are discarded when depleted.
with integraded grenade loader that holds 6 grenades, typically fragmentation grenades

Description: This specialized screen of charged particles produces protection against laser/beam attacks by absorbing them or reflecting them harmlessly away. A civilian reflec screen drains 2 EU per BP(B) absorbed. A security screen drains 1 EU per BP(B) absorbed. A military screen drains 1 EU per 2 BP(B) absorbed. Like all screens, they also drain 1 EU per minute. A reflec screen provides no special resistance or skill bonuses.

notes.p: A semi-autonomous bipedal unit with reactive armor plating and modular weapon hardpoints.

list.li: Armor, Military, quickly replaced armor plates, 1/2 total BP damage can be repaired in 1 hour
Improved security panel. Opening requires Technician check with -40 modifier unless passcode known.
Modular Hardpoints, integrated weapons can be swapped in 1 hour

notes.p: Marching and shooting
None
Travel: 5kph

Soldier bots are a fantastic replacement for actual soldiers. They don’t take breaks, need to eat or drink, or have consciences or object to your orders. They often fill the ranks of common troops and can help make a battle decisive and quick. They have no integrated weaponry of their own, they instead are trained in the use of all weapons and can be equipped in the same gear as the biological soldiers with whom they march.
Soldier bots aren't good at improvising. They carry out orders to the best of their ability, and generally can hit what they aim at. They’re far more durable than most biological soldiers.
They are often commanded by one or more war bots or biological sapient commanders. They are really good at killing enemies and marching in sync with one another, and that’s about it.

description.p: Used for all ability checks relating to fighting with fists, feet, or hand-held weapons. This is the skill of pugilists, brawlers, swordsmen, martial artists, guards, and knights. Sample specializations include sonic swords, vibro axes, electric stun rods, or unarmed combat.

This skill can be paired with different abilities, as described in the equipment tables in Chapter 5, depending on the weapon in question. Heavy weapons will rely on STR, while lighter weapons will rely on AGL. Small weapons wielded with finesse may even use CRD for determining chances to hit. Regardless of which weapon is used and which ability score is used, all melee weapons use the Warrior skill to hit. If the hit roll is successful, they will all receive the character’s Melee Damage Bonus, as determined during character creation.

One would think that in such an advanced technological society, skill with bare fists and swords would be less important. That’s just not the case. There are many situations where errant weaponry could breech hulls or cause collateral damage and therefore are far less useful. Additionally, many cultures throughout the frontier idolize such personal combat skill and teach it as a form of fitness, self-defense, and law enforcement.

This skill is added to ability checks relating to shooting or aiming, as well as training in the use of vehicle-mounted and other heavy or indirect weapons (although heavy and mounted weapons are fired at a penalty of -20). Sample specializations include specific weapon types such as semi-automatic pistols, automatic rifles, gyrojet rifles, laser pistols, thrown spears, crossbows, hurled grenades, etc. Don’t make the mistake of just thinking this skill is for shooting. Although that’s the most common use of it, it’s not the only one. When someone has the drop on you, a PER+Marksman action check might let you know if the weapon is loaded, or has its safety on. An INT+Marksman action check might help you figure out how to operate an alien weapon discovered on an ancient derelict ship. If your weapon is jammed (which might happen from critical failures), you might be able to clear it with a CRD+Marksman action check. Clever players can find other uses of this skill, approved by the Referee of course.

item_description.p: Description: This is basically a hand-held video recorder, able to take video and audio recordings in a narrow or wide-field mode. When used passively, it simply records all that is seen or heard. When used actively by draining 2 EU from the attached energy source, it takes an extraordinarily detailed holographic full-range snapshot/image, which it can store and send to a computer for more detailed 3-dimensional analysis.

Description: In passive mode, this hand-held scanner’s pads can be placed against something and will report its texture, temperature, and general safety level for biological beings. In many aspects, it’s more or less the equivalent of a biological being’s own hand and a bit less than useful, unless you’re a robot and want it integrated into your robot frame. When used actively (at a cost of 2 EU), the scanner sends out energy waves and reads their response on a very fine level, allowing it to “feel” something from a safe distance of up to 5 meters away.

id-00003.active: “see” up to 3 m depth on screen

item_description.p: Description: This hand-held x-ray machine can passively detect the presence of x-rays, but otherwise won’t do too much. When activated (drains 2 EU), however, it shows a picture on a small screen of an x-ray picture of up to 3 m depth. The x-rays can be harmful to biological beings if exposed for a prolonged time, but are mostly harmless in such a short burst. Although it won’t see through very dense metals, such as lead, it’s a great tool for prospectors looking for veins of valuables in mountains or by burglars and technicians searching within walls for power lines or security camera feeds.

id-00003.name: X-Ray Scanner
id-00003.passive: detect presence of x-rays, alert user

item_description.p: Connections: 1

Description: These are roughly the size of an old AA-sized battery and are interchangeable among all energy-draining devices, such as weapons and defenses. They provide 20 EU (“energy units”) of usable energy. EU devices are not rechargeable and are discarded when depleted.
Comms: Audio, video, text, holographic

Description: This is a heavier version of the communicator. It is a hand-held device for RF communications in a 100 km radius. Additionally, it includes a directional laser communications system that allows communication over nearly any distance as long as line of sight exists with the communications partner and he, too, has a long- range communicator. It also includes a satellite uplink system that interfaces with planetary communications satellites. It generally contains all the entertainment potential of a standard communicator and is powered by an array of microbatteries that last the lifetime of the equipment.

notes.p: Cargo hauling and rough terrain transport
Standard
Travel: 4kph

The Mulejack Loadwalker is a six-legged autonomous cargo hauler engineered for the unforgiving terrain of the Frontier. Designed by Karnex Industrial Robotics, it excels in transporting heavy loads across unstable ground, from jagged canyon passes to shifting ice fields. Its articulated limbs and adaptive gait algorithms allow it to maintain balance and traction even in zero-gravity or low-atmosphere conditions, making it indispensable for mining crews, survey expeditions, and remote outposts.

Equipped with modular cargo racks, environmental sensors, and a robust power core, the Mulejack can operate for weeks without maintenance. Its onboard AI prioritizes route optimization and load stability, ensuring that critical supplies—fuel cells, water tanks, excavation gear—arrive intact and on time. Though not flashy, the Mulejack has earned a reputation as the quiet workhorse of the Frontier: reliable, tireless, and built to endure where other machines falter.

description.p: This skill is added to ability checks relating to outdoorsmanship, handling animals, survival, hunting, tracking, terrestrial (ground-based) navigation, swimming, climbing trees or cliffs, etc. This skill is that of a guide, huntsman, recon scout, or survivalist. Example areas of specialization include animal handling, tracking, navigation, or survival in specific type of terrain or dominant weather/environmental conditions (tundra, jungle, desert, steppes, canyons, marshes, etc.). Many of the uses of the Explorer skill requires specialized tools or materials. These are abstracted into an Explorer Kit, found and described in the Equipment & Technology chapter. When provision points run out, an Explorer may be limited in what he may be able to accomplish!

This skill is added to ability checks relating to repairing things, engineering with electronic, mechanical, robotic, and computer systems. It is the skill of the mechanic, engineer, programmer, roboticist, or hacker. Most of what you can do with this skill requires a techkit, which has a limited number of provision points for working your trade. You’ll have to make sure you keep your techkit provisioned for adventures.
When you select this skill, you must specialize in one facet of technical expertise chosen from the list below (or some other technological field permitted by the Referee).

list.li: Computers : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of all types of computers and computerized hand-held equipment in the frontier.
Robots : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of all types of robots and robotic industrial equipment.

Starships : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of all types of space-faring vessels and vehicles (from shuttles and workpods to cruisers, and everything in between).
Structures : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of buildings, bridges, walls, bunkers, and other structural objects.
Vehicles : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of all types of vehicles (land, sea, hover, air, etc.).

item_description.p: Description: This is basically a hand-held video recorder, able to take video and audio recordings in a narrow or wide-field mode. When used passively, it simply records all that is seen or heard. When used actively by draining 2 EU from the attached energy source, it takes an extraordinarily detailed holographic full-range snapshot/image, which it can store and send to a computer for more detailed 3-dimensional analysis.

Description: The passive use of this hand-held device gives voice to the hand that holds it. The handle reads the bioelectric field generated by the user and, with practice, can turn thought into audio. This is used by mutes in the galaxy to allow them a voice where they have none of their own. It’s also common to integrate with artificial intelligences, such as robots, to give them the ability to speak. Vocal scanners are programmed with many languages and can be activated (drains 2 EU) to enable translator circuits to allow a character’s thoughts to be spoken in another galactic standard language, though it won’t help a character understand what is spoken back.

Description: A hand-held device that uses radar, ladar, thermal, sonar, and passive air-density sensors to locate unseen moving objects in fog, darkness, behind thin obstacles, etc. It registers movement in a 1 km radius, so it will not detect a stationary robot, vehicle, or person. Algorithms attempt to identify each moving object with text on the device’s display, and the interface allows additional information to be queried (such as a blip’s speed and heading, estimated time of arrival, etc.). An active scan (drains 2 EU) increases the radius to 10 km, but only for one turn to get a momentary snapshot of longer range motion. It can get expensive to continue spending 2 EU per turn to track motion for a long-range encounter. Since motion scanners rely on a host of different technologies, it’s nearly impossible to completely mask oneself from its scan, but someone equipped with an Infrared jammer, ComJammer, and Sonic jammer has a 50% chance per turn of not showing up on the motion scanner.

Description: These specialized goggles allow a character to see in the infrared spectrum. This allows him to see in total darkness for 20 m by seeing the heat signatures of objects nearby. The wearer cannot see with enough detail to read (the page mostly looks the same color), but they allow him to see people quite easily. Characters can use these to ignore the obscuring effect of a smoke grenade.

Description: These digital magnification goggles can be controlled to zoom up to 10x distance. They include electronic range finding algorithms to permit the wearer a firm understanding of the distance to a target he’s watching. They’re very common with scouts and explorers.
Connections: 8

Description: These large cylinders hold a vast 1,000 EU. They are not very portable, requiring two men to carry using extendable handles (though it can also be rolled by one man if terrain permits). EU drums are normally loaded into starships and capital ships as ammunition stores for beam weapons, but might also be located at a military base powering a heavy laser or large projection screen. EU drums have eight retractable cables, allowing them to connect to more than one device. EU devices are not rechargeable and are discarded when depleted.

Description: This kinetic screen of forced particle pressures produces protection against physical/ kinetic attacks, such as from falling, swords, knives, bullets, etc. A civilian ballistic screen drains 2 EU per BP absorbed. A security screen drains 1 EU per BP absorbed. A military screen drains 1 EU per 2 BP absorbed. Prolonged use of screens drains 1 EU per minute. A ballistic screen provides no special resistance or skill bonuses.

Description: This specialized screen of charged particles produces protection against laser/beam attacks by absorbing them or reflecting them harmlessly away. A civilian reflec screen drains 2 EU per BP(B) absorbed. A security screen drains 1 EU per BP(B) absorbed. A military screen drains 1 EU per 2 BP(B) absorbed. Like all screens, they also drain 1 EU per minute. A reflec screen provides no special resistance or skill bonuses.
Comms: Audio, video, text, holographic

Description: This is by far the most popular communication device in the known galaxy. There are many brands, and some have features others do not. A communicator is a hand-held device consisting mostly of a durable touch screen. Its range is 10 km when using radio frequency (RF) signals, or virtually unlimited when connected to a digital satellite network common to civilized regions. Communicators can be encrypted for private conversations and can include audio, visual, holographic, and text components. Various entertainment applications can be downloaded while on digital networks for a low cost. Communicators are powered by microbatteries, which generally last the lifetime of the equipment.

Description: This heavy laser weapon has an array of five barrels that fire in tandem to create one large violet beam. It is air- and water-cooled and has a lot of bulk to it. The weapon can be set to a range of power levels: 1-5. It drains 10 EU from the power source and causes 1Dx10(B) damage per power level setting. For instance, power level 1 drains 10 EU from the attached source and causes 1Dx10(B) damage, while power level 5 drains 50 EU and causes 5Dx10(B) damage. The weapon has a very large range and can tear up most vehicles or walls with a shot or two. A heavy laser is a powerful deterrent. Any vehicle or military outpost with an energy drum and a heavy laser can cause amazing levels of devastation, and can even cause damage to hull point structures such as shuttles and starfighters.
A heavy laser often controls the battlefield.

Description: This large weapon is fed by a long belt of 200 machinegun bullets chained together with special linkage. The bullets are fired through a very long barrel, where they pick up great speed and stopping power. Machineguns are cost effective compared to heavy lasers and popular because of it. They fire only in burstfire mode (having no semiautomatic mode). Every burst offers a +20 bonus (but remember that unless the wielder has the Gunnery skill benefit, he will be at a -20 to hit) and uses 10 bullets. A burst from a machinegun causes 2Dx10 damage and is a great anti- personnel or anti-light-vehicle weapon.

Description: This is a large box on a turret, which can be aimed using electronic targeting software connected to a gimble. The missiles are fired one at a time, but the rack holds 4 at a time. Each is reloaded individually. The missiles have a truly impressive range and when they hit cause 8Dx10 explosive damage. The devastation a missile causes is quite satisfying. If they hit, they can generally be relied on to destroy what they hit.

notes.p: A semi-autonomous bipedal unit with reactive armor plating and modular weapon hardpoints.

list.li: Armor, Military, quickly replaced armor plates, 1/2 total BP damage can be repaired in 1 hour
Improved security panel. Opening requires Technician check with -40 modifier unless passcode known.
Modular Hardpoints, integrated weapons can be swapped in 1 hour

notes.p: Killing people and breaking things
Um... no.

list.li: Upper body is swiveling turret with all weapons

notes.p: Travel: 10kph

Weighing in at several tons and standing 2.5 m tall, this four-legged combatant is nothing a lone adventurer wants to run afoul of. Armed to the teeth and as durable as they come, a platoon of six of these can hold off a conventional Human squad of 50 or more if the situation allows. It runs across the battlefield, often strafing at targets while remaining mobile. It coordinates soldier bots in large scale tactical warfare and is fearsome to behold. The robot design itself resembles a walking tank more than a robot, with no discernable centralized sensor cluster or head processing unit, everything is protected and encased.

Some warbot manufacturers have a violence interlock for one programmable species (specified upon purchase). Therefore, soldiers can work side- by-side with the warbot and rest assured the machine cannot harm them.  WAR BOT Fortunately, the high cost of the war bot prevents it from seeing wide-spread use in societies not currently engaged in war. When used, they are usually decommissioned after conflict. Despite this, you can find them enforcing religious doctrine on Orgayn (Asimaar System) and Kingsport (Krighton System) wearing the banner colors of the prelate’s family. Some witnesses even report seeing some old war bots still roaming the desert environments of Scaramoore in the Undecia System, remnants of battles which plagued Reinaggar’s Hold history.
Note that the statistics shown in the stat block are for common, modern war bots. In many cases, the heavy laser, machinegun, and missile rack are replaced by other heavy weapon configurations.

description.p: This skill is added to ability checks relating to shooting or aiming, as well as training in the use of vehicle-mounted and other heavy or indirect weapons (although heavy and mounted weapons are fired at a penalty of -20). Sample specializations include specific weapon types such as semi-automatic pistols, automatic rifles, gyrojet rifles, laser pistols, thrown spears, crossbows, hurled grenades, etc. Don’t make the mistake of just thinking this skill is for shooting. Although that’s the most common use of it, it’s not the only one. When someone has the drop on you, a PER+Marksman action check might let you know if the weapon is loaded, or has its safety on. An INT+Marksman action check might help you figure out how to operate an alien weapon discovered on an ancient derelict ship. If your weapon is jammed (which might happen from critical failures), you might be able to clear it with a CRD+Marksman action check. Clever players can find other uses of this skill, approved by the Referee of course.

Used for all ability checks relating to fighting with fists, feet, or hand-held weapons. This is the skill of pugilists, brawlers, swordsmen, martial artists, guards, and knights. Sample specializations include sonic swords, vibro axes, electric stun rods, or unarmed combat.

This skill can be paired with different abilities, as described in the equipment tables in Chapter 5, depending on the weapon in question. Heavy weapons will rely on STR, while lighter weapons will rely on AGL. Small weapons wielded with finesse may even use CRD for determining chances to hit. Regardless of which weapon is used and which ability score is used, all melee weapons use the Warrior skill to hit. If the hit roll is successful, they will all receive the character’s Melee Damage Bonus, as determined during character creation.

One would think that in such an advanced technological society, skill with bare fists and swords would be less important. That’s just not the case. There are many situations where errant weaponry could breech hulls or cause collateral damage and therefore are far less useful. Additionally, many cultures throughout the frontier idolize such personal combat skill and teach it as a form of fitness, self-defense, and law enforcement.

This score is used for ability checks relating to strategy and tactics, or leading a team of characters in stressful situations. The most common use of the Commander skill is to figure out tactical situations that might lead to benefits, such as finding the best ambush spot, or detecting when you’re entering an ideal spot for such an ambush. But additional uses include finding weaknesses in enemy defenses, bolstering morale of those you lead, etc. Sample areas of specialization include small unit tactics, armada strategies, naval battles, guerilla warfare, or perhaps command of battles in specific terrain, such as jungles, urban, desert, etc.

The Commander skill requires special handling by the Referee since successful use is meant to modify the player character’s chance of survival in a tight spot. This skill should not be confused with the Diplomat skill; both have very different uses.

Some examples of benefits you might expect from successful Commander checks include bolster morale of those you lead: +20 on WIL checks, spot enemy's weakness: all the attacks from your allies in the following turn +2D to damage, etc.

item_description.p: Description: This is basically a hand-held video recorder, able to take video and audio recordings in a narrow or wide-field mode. When used passively, it simply records all that is seen or heard. When used actively by draining 2 EU from the attached energy source, it takes an extraordinarily detailed holographic full-range snapshot/image, which it can store and send to a computer for more detailed 3-dimensional analysis.
