The handle reads the bioelectric field generated by the user and, with practice, can turn thought into audio. This is used by mutes in the galaxy to allow them a voice where they have none of their own. It’s also common to integrate with artificial intelligences, such as robots, to give them the ability to speak. Vocal scanners are programmed with many languages and can be activated (drains 2 EU) to enable translator circuits to allow a character’s thoughts to be spoken in another galactic standard language, though it won’t help a character understand what is spoken back.

id-00003.active: cuts time in half when using medic skill

item_description.p: Description: The only passive use this hand-held device has is as a sophisticated medical tablet, with updated medical journals and published study results, pharmaceutical dosage and interaction guides, and various other medical texts helpful to a doctor. When used actively (drains 2 EU), it can perform a very comprehensive health scan of a subject. The results help by cutting in half the time it takes to perform a diagnosis, treatment, or surgery of the patient. Medical scanners are commonly carried by doctors on exploration missions.

id-00003.name: Medical Scanner

item_description.p: Description: This hand-held device can be used passively to see if a living being is dead or if something is actually a living being, but otherwise its passive use is limited. When used actively (drains 2 EU), it produces a multi-wavelength ping (that has a known and identifiable audible sound) to scan for all living things within 10 m. Internal analysis software attempts to identify it based on analyses of the return signals and displays it all on the small screen. The lifeform data can be used to record a lot about a lifeform, and might help a medical doctor learn more about it. For this reason, many exploration and first contact teams carry a lifeform scanner.

id-00004.name: Lifeform Scanner
id-00004.passive: scan being to see if alive

id-00005.active: “see” up to 3 m depth on screen

item_description.p: Description: This hand-held x-ray machine can passively detect the presence of x-rays, but otherwise won’t do too much. When activated (drains 2 EU), however, it shows a picture on a small screen of an x-ray picture of up to 3 m depth. The x-rays can be harmful to biological beings if exposed for a prolonged time, but are mostly harmless in such a short burst. Although it won’t see through very dense metals, such as lead, it’s a great tool for prospectors looking for veins of valuables in mountains or by burglars and technicians searching within walls for power lines or security camera feeds.

id-00005.name: X-Ray Scanner
id-00005.passive: detect presence of x-rays, alert user

item_description.p: Training: Medic

Description: The medkit exists to abstract the many things that a capable medic must carry around to have a chance to treat diseases, poisons, and perform field surgery. It has a variety of bandages, scalpels, spray cans of plasti-flesh, antibiotics, stimulants, anesthetics, inoculants, spray hypos, common anti-venoms, single-use disposable litmus strips and dishes, and much more. They come with 20 points of medical provisions. Each time a character performs an action check requiring his medkit, it depletes 1 or more of these provisions. Surgery and first aid drain 1 provision point for every 5 BP (or fraction thereof) healed. Most other types of medical action checks (diagnosing and treating ailments) drain 1. When the medkit is out of points, the medic is unable to help people effectively.
Connections: 2

Description: These are approximately the size of a fat wallet or small belt pouch. They possess two retractable cables ending in connectors the size of a power pack, allowing them to power two different devices simultaneously. They provide 50 EU of usable energy. EU devices are not rechargeable and are discarded when depleted.

Description: These provide extra Body Points to protect the wearer from physical damage. They do not protect against beam, electric, or sonic damage, but will protect against the most common types of damage, such as from bullets, swords, and the claws of beasts. Once the suit is reduced to 0 BP, any additional damage applies to the character and the suit is trashed. A ballistic suit can be repaired by a technician with a techkit, but only if it has 1 or more BP left.

Civilian suits provide only 10 BP but are totally indistinguishable from normal clothes. Security suits provide 25 BP and appear as light body armor or paramilitary or security uniforms, though they can be concealed beneath long jackets or bulky clothes. Military suits offer 50 BP but cannot be hidden; it’s obvious the character is wearing body armor.

notes.p: A humanoid medical assistant capable of battlefield triage and surgical procedures.
Saving lives, advising good health
Standard
Travel: 5 kph

From the battlefield to the starship sick bay, medical robots are found throughout the galaxy, tending to the wounded or assisting skilled surgeons in complex procedures. Although they lack the imagination to solve truly complex medical problems, they are very efficient at tackling more mundane medical tasks, such as treating common viruses, standardized surgical procedures, patching up injuries, and prescribing common medicines for known maladies.

The first time you lay your eyes on a medical bot, it might be a little intimidating. They’re built strong and sturdy enough to carry an unconscious soldier off a battlefield, and have four arms and hands so they may assist themselves in medical tasks. Despite this, they’re otherwise built with two legs and a head unit where their sensors are clustered, so they can fit/go where people fit/go.

description.p: This skill is added to ability checks relating to first aid, diagnosing and treating toxins and diseases, and performing field surgery. Most of what you can do with this skill requires a certain amount of time (determined by the Referee) and a medkit, which has a limited number of provision points for working your trade. You’ll have to make sure you keep your medkit provisioned for adventures.

The Medic skill isn’t just for patching up your friends when they get shot up. You can also use it to diagnose maladies, treat diseases, cure poisons, treat radiation exposure, stimulate unconscious characters to wake them up in the middle of a fight, save a dying character, or to perform other creative uses of medicines as arbitrated by your Referee. In many ways, the medic’s medkit can be as versatile as a fantasy RPG alchemist’s kit, given the right circumstances.

Refer to the equipment chapter for more about the medkit, and see the summary below. Sample specializations include specific species such as Erakai, Human, Novim, or Yar, or could refer to specific medical specialties such as cardiology, cybernetics, immunology, genetics, etc.

This skill represents the analysis and classification of various facets of the universe and includes a very broad array of various types of sciences. All fields of science represent application of the scientific method to break down and understand the relationships of things in the frontier.
When you select this skill, you must specialize in one facet of scientific expertise , such as those shown below (or some other academic field permitted by the Referee).

list.li: Life Science: This specialization is the study of how biological subjects function and interact with one another and their environments. It represents such fields of science as botany, zoology, biology, psychology, xenobiology, ecology, etc.

Physical Science : This specialization is the study of the physical universe and how particles and chemistries exist and interact with one another. It represents such fields of science as physics, chemistry, photonics, aeronautics, mathematics, thermodynamics, astrogation, etc.

Planetary Science: This specialization is the study of environmental and planetary forces, such as hydrology, oceanography, mineralogy, geology, meteorology, climatology, etc. It is the preferred tool of the planetary surveyor.

description.p: This skill is added to ability checks relating to researching or recalling learned knowledge, communicating without a common language, interacting with complex cultures, dealing with legal or political matters, understanding literature, and dealing with other facets of scholarly aptitude.
When you select this skill, you must specialize in one facet of academic expertise , such as those shown below (or some other academic field permitted by the Referee).

list.li: Business/Economics : This specialization deals with the ins and outs of starting and running a business, trade, or service successfully in the frontier.

History/Cultures : This specialization deals with the lessons of the past, histories of various species and cultures, and understanding how those cultures existed and interacted with one another across the temporal landscape.

Linguistics : This specialization deals with the study of language and its structure, morphology, syntax, phonetics, and semantics. A character can spend time with an unknown written language and translate it by use of this skill, and a successful action check might reduce the DP cost to learn a language by 1. See Chapter 6: Character Development for more information.

Literature/Poetry : This specialization deals with the study of written works of art. It’s not only a scholarly appreciation for these, but a deeper understanding into the causes and results of such literature and poetry.
Politics/Law: This specialization deals with the political landscape and the policies, practices, and laws which define it. A specialist might be a lawyer, judge, or even a politician.
Theology/Philosophy : This specialization deals with the many philosophies and religions of the frontier, as well as how they interact with one another.

item_description.p: Description: This is basically a hand-held video recorder, able to take video and audio recordings in a narrow or wide-field mode. When used passively, it simply records all that is seen or heard. When used actively by draining 2 EU from the attached energy source, it takes an extraordinarily detailed holographic full-range snapshot/image, which it can store and send to a computer for more detailed 3-dimensional analysis.

Description: The passive use of this hand-held device gives voice to the hand that holds it. The handle reads the bioelectric field generated by the user and, with practice, can turn thought into audio. This is used by mutes in the galaxy to allow them a voice where they have none of their own. It’s also common to integrate with artificial intelligences, such as robots, to give them the ability to speak. Vocal scanners are programmed with many languages and can be activated (drains 2 EU) to enable translator circuits to allow a character’s thoughts to be spoken in another galactic standard language, though it won’t help a character understand what is spoken back.
Comms: Audio, video, text, holographic

Description: This is by far the most popular communication device in the known galaxy. There are many brands, and some have features others do not. A communicator is a hand-held device consisting mostly of a durable touch screen. Its range is 10 km when using radio frequency (RF) signals, or virtually unlimited when connected to a digital satellite network common to civilized regions. Communicators can be encrypted for private conversations and can include audio, visual, holographic, and text components. Various entertainment applications can be downloaded while on digital networks for a low cost. Communicators are powered by microbatteries, which generally last the lifetime of the equipment.

notes.p: A floating, voice-modulating liaison bot used in tense negotiations and covert diplomacy.

list.li: Universal linguist. Robot knows all languages and dialects known in the frontier at time of construction.
Improved security panel. Opening requires Technician check with -40 modifier unless passcode known.
Designer model. Worth 50% more value, mostly due to its name. Appearance and paint job is impressive, but that doesn’t make it any more effective at what it does.

notes.p: Specific to programmed profession
Standard
Travel: 100 kph

Protocol bots were originally engineered as messengers, to ferry letters, data, or small packages around a city. Over the years they became very popular among robot enthusiasts because of their easy-to-program nature, large integration space, and vast memory capacity.

Today, you can find protocol bots programmed for just about every profession. They are seldom found without a vast database of cultures and locations. Their long service time has often given them interesting personalities. They hover around at chest-height to most Humans and have retractable arms often equipped with profession- specific tools.
Protocol bots are often purchased by adventurers needing an extra teammate to round out a few skills the adventurers lack in their team.

description.p: This skill is added to ability checks relating to social dealings with others. It’s the skill of persuasion, wit, charm, seduction, etiquette, swindling, haggling, negotiation, interrogation, and more. It’s also used when resisting such attempts by others. A character who wants to be able to lead others through tactics and strategy chooses to increase his Commander skill. A character who wants to control commanders, and perhaps the leaders of those they battle, chooses to increase his Diplomat skill. Smart leaders do both. Characters can specialize in individual aspects of diplomacy (wit, charm, seduction, haggling, etc.) or with specific social groups (political figures, corporate executives, criminals, etc.).

This skill is added to ability checks relating to researching or recalling learned knowledge, communicating without a common language, interacting with complex cultures, dealing with legal or political matters, understanding literature, and dealing with other facets of scholarly aptitude.
When you select this skill, you must specialize in one facet of academic expertise , such as those shown below (or some other academic field permitted by the Referee).

list.li: Business/Economics : This specialization deals with the ins and outs of starting and running a business, trade, or service successfully in the frontier.

History/Cultures : This specialization deals with the lessons of the past, histories of various species and cultures, and understanding how those cultures existed and interacted with one another across the temporal landscape.

Linguistics : This specialization deals with the study of language and its structure, morphology, syntax, phonetics, and semantics. A character can spend time with an unknown written language and translate it by use of this skill, and a successful action check might reduce the DP cost to learn a language by 1. See Chapter 6: Character Development for more information.

Literature/Poetry : This specialization deals with the study of written works of art. It’s not only a scholarly appreciation for these, but a deeper understanding into the causes and results of such literature and poetry.
Politics/Law: This specialization deals with the political landscape and the policies, practices, and laws which define it. A specialist might be a lawyer, judge, or even a politician.
Theology/Philosophy : This specialization deals with the many philosophies and religions of the frontier, as well as how they interact with one another.

description.p: This skill represents the analysis and classification of various facets of the universe and includes a very broad array of various types of sciences. All fields of science represent application of the scientific method to break down and understand the relationships of things in the frontier.
When you select this skill, you must specialize in one facet of scientific expertise , such as those shown below (or some other academic field permitted by the Referee).

list.li: Life Science: This specialization is the study of how biological subjects function and interact with one another and their environments. It represents such fields of science as botany, zoology, biology, psychology, xenobiology, ecology, etc.

Physical Science : This specialization is the study of the physical universe and how particles and chemistries exist and interact with one another. It represents such fields of science as physics, chemistry, photonics, aeronautics, mathematics, thermodynamics, astrogation, etc.

Planetary Science: This specialization is the study of environmental and planetary forces, such as hydrology, oceanography, mineralogy, geology, meteorology, climatology, etc. It is the preferred tool of the planetary surveyor.
Some type of rifle is typical as a primary weapon
Some type of pistol sidearm as a backup

item_description.p: Comms: Audio, text

Description: This is a device worn on the wrist. It functions as a very modern digital watch and includes an RF radio with a range of 5 km. It’s audio only, but that is normally sufficient. On a civilized world, it will connect to digital communication networks and allow unlimited range. Wristcoms are nearly as popular as hand- held communicators, and are powered by the same microbatteries designed to last the lifetime of ownership. If the Referee permits, wristcoms can come in other forms which allow hands-free audio-only communication, such as earpieces with boom-mics or badges worn on the chest with an included insignia.

Description: This is a heavier rifle-sized version of the auto pistol. It fires the same ammunition (it’s interchangeable), but due to the longer barrel it has a superior range and generates more speed upon discharge. The box magazine holds more bullets as well. Like the auto pistol, this rifle can be set to semiautomatic or burstfire mode. In semiautomatic mode, it fires one bullet every time it’s used, which causes 3D damage. In burstfire mode, it fires a burst of 5 bullets as one attack, offering +20 to hit and causing 6D damage. This weapon is very durable and is used by most armies throughout the galaxy due to the inexpensiveness of the ammunition.

Description: This is basically a hand-held video recorder, able to take video and audio recordings in a narrow or wide-field mode. When used passively, it simply records all that is seen or heard. When used actively by draining 2 EU from the attached energy source, it takes an extraordinarily detailed holographic full-range snapshot/image, which it can store and send to a computer for more detailed 3-dimensional analysis.

Description: The passive use of this hand-held device gives voice to the hand that holds it. The handle reads the bioelectric field generated by the user and, with practice, can turn thought into audio. This is used by mutes in the galaxy to allow them a voice where they have none of their own. It’s also common to integrate with artificial intelligences, such as robots, to give them the ability to speak. Vocal scanners are programmed with many languages and can be activated (drains 2 EU) to enable translator circuits to allow a character’s thoughts to be spoken in another galactic standard language, though it won’t help a character understand what is spoken back.
Connections: 4

Description: These are large but flat backpacks, hardened and sturdy. They’re more commonly used by wealthy explorers or laser-equipped military units. They have four retractable cables allowing them to connect to up to four devices and provide 100 EU of energy. EU devices are not rechargeable and are discarded when depleted.

Description: Not everyone can afford to shell out hundreds of credits for a laser pistol, but many want the advantages of modern beam weaponry. For these people, the slightly lower-tech plasma- based blastpistol is just the right tool for the job. It is a little bulky, but it does pack quite a punch. When fired, the energy from its EU source is focused through inexpensive crystal membranes and releases in a rather large, nasty red blast that sounds thunderous rather than elegant. It’s followed by a high-pitched whine while the crystal membranes perform a quick regeneration. It is not a stealthy pistol. Each shot drains 2 EU from its energy source and causes 2D(B) damage.

Description: To keep things simple, bullet-based ammunition is purchased in pre-packaged and tested boxed magazines. The bullets themselves are interchangeable, even though they’re used for different types of weapons. Players who find magazines of different sizes than those they need can always remove bullets from one and add to another. This is especially helpful when scavenging the partially depleted magazines of your enemies. Small 10-bullet magazines are used for semiauto pistols. Medium-sized 20-bullet magazines are used for auto pistols, semiauto rifles, and sniper rifles. Long 30-bullet magazines are used for auto rifles.

Description: This specialized screen of charged particles produces protection against laser/beam attacks by absorbing them or reflecting them harmlessly away. A civilian reflec screen drains 2 EU per BP(B) absorbed. A security screen drains 1 EU per BP(B) absorbed. A military screen drains 1 EU per 2 BP(B) absorbed. Like all screens, they also drain 1 EU per minute. A reflec screen provides no special resistance or skill bonuses.
Connections: 1

Description: These are roughly the size of an old AA-sized battery and are interchangeable among all energy-draining devices, such as weapons and defenses. They provide 20 EU (“energy units”) of usable energy. EU devices are not rechargeable and are discarded when depleted.

list.li: Advantage with all STR-based action checks. Robot has extraordinary grip.
Disadvantage with all PER-based action checks. Poorly integrated scanner subsystem.

notes.p: Marching and shooting
None
Travel: 5kph

Soldier bots are a fantastic replacement for actual soldiers. They don’t take breaks, need to eat or drink, or have consciences or object to your orders. They often fill the ranks of common troops and can help make a battle decisive and quick. They have no integrated weaponry of their own, they instead are trained in the use of all weapons and can be equipped in the same gear as the biological soldiers with whom they march.
Soldier bots aren't good at improvising. They carry out orders to the best of their ability, and generally can hit what they aim at. They’re far more durable than most biological soldiers.
They are often commanded by one or more war bots or biological sapient commanders. They are really good at killing enemies and marching in sync with one another, and that’s about it.

description.p: Used for all ability checks relating to fighting with fists, feet, or hand-held weapons. This is the skill of pugilists, brawlers, swordsmen, martial artists, guards, and knights. Sample specializations include sonic swords, vibro axes, electric stun rods, or unarmed combat.

This skill can be paired with different abilities, as described in the equipment tables in Chapter 5, depending on the weapon in question. Heavy weapons will rely on STR, while lighter weapons will rely on AGL. Small weapons wielded with finesse may even use CRD for determining chances to hit. Regardless of which weapon is used and which ability score is used, all melee weapons use the Warrior skill to hit. If the hit roll is successful, they will all receive the character’s Melee Damage Bonus, as determined during character creation.

One would think that in such an advanced technological society, skill with bare fists and swords would be less important. That’s just not the case. There are many situations where errant weaponry could breech hulls or cause collateral damage and therefore are far less useful. Additionally, many cultures throughout the frontier idolize such personal combat skill and teach it as a form of fitness, self-defense, and law enforcement.

This skill is added to ability checks relating to shooting or aiming, as well as training in the use of vehicle-mounted and other heavy or indirect weapons (although heavy and mounted weapons are fired at a penalty of -20). Sample specializations include specific weapon types such as semi-automatic pistols, automatic rifles, gyrojet rifles, laser pistols, thrown spears, crossbows, hurled grenades, etc. Don’t make the mistake of just thinking this skill is for shooting. Although that’s the most common use of it, it’s not the only one. When someone has the drop on you, a PER+Marksman action check might let you know if the weapon is loaded, or has its safety on. An INT+Marksman action check might help you figure out how to operate an alien weapon discovered on an ancient derelict ship. If your weapon is jammed (which might happen from critical failures), you might be able to clear it with a CRD+Marksman action check. Clever players can find other uses of this skill, approved by the Referee of course.

item_description.p: Description: This is basically a hand-held video recorder, able to take video and audio recordings in a narrow or wide-field mode. When used passively, it simply records all that is seen or heard. When used actively by draining 2 EU from the attached energy source, it takes an extraordinarily detailed holographic full-range snapshot/image, which it can store and send to a computer for more detailed 3-dimensional analysis.

Description: When used passively, this hand-held device functions like a communicator. But when used actively (drains 2 EU), it performs a comprehensive scan of all wavelengths, looking for various types of amplitude, frequency, and phase modulations which might identify communication channels. It can actively demodulate these (unless they’re encoded) and produce audio for all to hear. If it detects video in the signal, it can show the video as well. As part of the active scan, it will indicate the direction of the signal’s source, and attempt to calculate the distance to its point of origin.

Description: These specialized goggles allow a character to see in the infrared spectrum. This allows him to see in total darkness for 20 m by seeing the heat signatures of objects nearby. The wearer cannot see with enough detail to read (the page mostly looks the same color), but they allow him to see people quite easily. Characters can use these to ignore the obscuring effect of a smoke grenade.
Connections: 1

Description: These are roughly the size of an old AA-sized battery and are interchangeable among all energy-draining devices, such as weapons and defenses. They provide 20 EU (“energy units”) of usable energy. EU devices are not rechargeable and are discarded when depleted.
Training: Thief

Description: This is a rolled-up strip of leather or canvas containing dozens of pouches and all the physical and electronic tools a burglar needs to pick locks and crack safes. Without a lock pick set, characters won’t be able to use the Thief skill to get past locked doors, electronic or otherwise.
Comms: Audio, video, text, holographic

Description: This is by far the most popular communication device in the known galaxy. There are many brands, and some have features others do not. A communicator is a hand-held device consisting mostly of a durable touch screen. Its range is 10 km when using radio frequency (RF) signals, or virtually unlimited when connected to a digital satellite network common to civilized regions. Communicators can be encrypted for private conversations and can include audio, visual, holographic, and text components. Various entertainment applications can be downloaded while on digital networks for a low cost. Communicators are powered by microbatteries, which generally last the lifetime of the equipment.

notes.p: A swarm-capable surveillance bot that resembles a mechanical beetle and can self-destruct on command.

list.li: Exposed sensor cluster. If robot receives critical hit, it loses all scanners and is blinded, deafened, etc.
Disadvantage with all STR-based action checks. The robot’s structural system is light.
Command Destruct, 5D explosion in a 5M radius

notes.p: Seeking, scanning, finding
Modified

list.li: Design to look like a larkge insect

notes.p: Travel: 75kph

Much less capable in most respects to a seeker bot, they serve a similar function, but have the added capability of being able to be remotely detonated. Swarms of these bots can be deadly if used in such a manner.
These amazing robots are tiny and capable. They can be mistaken for a large insectoid. They have

description.p: This skill is added to ability checks relating to illicit activity, such as sneaking, hiding, lock picking, defeating security systems, disguise, forgery, etc. This is a skill for spies, rogues, burglars, imposters, and outlaws. It is also the skill of a detective who might use it, along with PER or INT, to solve crimes, though if that detective puts his training to nefarious purpose he’d be a formidable rogue himself. Specializations include prowling, hiding, scaling buildings, picking locks, cracking safes, streetwise, bypassing security systems, disguise, counterfeiting, forgery, etc.

A Thief skill is generally not used when trying to lie to someone – that (along with other social interactions) belongs to the Diplomat skill. The Thief skill might be used to disguise as or mimic another person, however, but once the disguise or mimicry is successful, it’s the Diplomat skill that is used to socially interact with people. Hackers are a type of thief in conceptual terms, but generally won’t use the Thief skill to accomplish such techno-burglary.

Hackers usually use the Technician skill with a Computers specialization. However, it’s the Referee’s job to adjudicate which skill is used for what kind of situation, and there may be some cases where the Thief skill could be handy for such a hacker profession.

This skill represents the analysis and classification of various facets of the universe and includes a very broad array of various types of sciences. All fields of science represent application of the scientific method to break down and understand the relationships of things in the frontier.
When you select this skill, you must specialize in one facet of scientific expertise , such as those shown below (or some other academic field permitted by the Referee).

list.li: Life Science: This specialization is the study of how biological subjects function and interact with one another and their environments. It represents such fields of science as botany, zoology, biology, psychology, xenobiology, ecology, etc.

Physical Science : This specialization is the study of the physical universe and how particles and chemistries exist and interact with one another. It represents such fields of science as physics, chemistry, photonics, aeronautics, mathematics, thermodynamics, astrogation, etc.

Planetary Science: This specialization is the study of environmental and planetary forces, such as hydrology, oceanography, mineralogy, geology, meteorology, climatology, etc. It is the preferred tool of the planetary surveyor.

When used passively, it simply records all that is seen or heard. When used actively by draining 2 EU from the attached energy source, it takes an extraordinarily detailed holographic full-range snapshot/image, which it can store and send to a computer for more detailed 3-dimensional analysis.

Description: The passive use of this hand-held device gives voice to the hand that holds it. The handle reads the bioelectric field generated by the user and, with practice, can turn thought into audio. This is used by mutes in the galaxy to allow them a voice where they have none of their own. It’s also common to integrate with artificial intelligences, such as robots, to give them the ability to speak. Vocal scanners are programmed with many languages and can be activated (drains 2 EU) to enable translator circuits to allow a character’s thoughts to be spoken in another galactic standard language, though it won’t help a character understand what is spoken back.

Description: In passive mode, this hand-held scanner’s pads can be placed against something and will report its texture, temperature, and general safety level for biological beings. In many aspects, it’s more or less the equivalent of a biological being’s own hand and a bit less than useful, unless you’re a robot and want it integrated into your robot frame. When used actively (at a cost of 2 EU), the scanner sends out energy waves and reads their response on a very fine level, allowing it to “feel” something from a safe distance of up to 5 meters away.
Comms: Audio, text

Description: This is a device worn on the wrist. It functions as a very modern digital watch and includes an RF radio with a range of 5 km. It’s audio only, but that is normally sufficient. On a civilized world, it will connect to digital communication networks and allow unlimited range. Wristcoms are nearly as popular as hand- held communicators, and are powered by the same microbatteries designed to last the lifetime of ownership. If the Referee permits, wristcoms can come in other forms which allow hands-free audio-only communication, such as earpieces with boom-mics or badges worn on the chest with an included insignia.

notes.p: A cheerful assistant bot that thrives on organizing chaos and lending a mechanical hand.

list.li: Not water-safe. If submersed in water, circuitry fails and suffer from normal “drowning” rules.
Loud servo system. All of the robot’s movements are accompanied by a slight electrical noise.

notes.p: Assistance, food preparation, sanitation
Standard
Travel: 5kph

There are many types of service bots providing domestic and professional services from food preparation to house cleaning to child rearing. They are probably the most commonly sold type of robot in the galaxy.

Service bots will allow themselves great injury to keep the people they serve safe and healthy. They were designed for parents to be able to trust their children in their care. The service bot can even be asked to pick the children up from school and knows how to operate most domestic vehicles.

They are designed with very likeable faces and have tactile feedback built into their bodies to let them test things for temperature or safety before allowing a person to do the same. They are programmed to appear to care about the people in their charge. Who are we to say it’s artificial?

description.p: This skill is added to ability checks relating to researching or recalling learned knowledge, communicating without a common language, interacting with complex cultures, dealing with legal or political matters, understanding literature, and dealing with other facets of scholarly aptitude.
When you select this skill, you must specialize in one facet of academic expertise , such as those shown below (or some other academic field permitted by the Referee).

list.li: Life Science: This specialization is the study of how biological subjects function and interact with one another and their environments. It represents such fields of science as botany, zoology, biology, psychology, xenobiology, ecology, etc.

Physical Science : This specialization is the study of the physical universe and how particles and chemistries exist and interact with one another. It represents such fields of science as physics, chemistry, photonics, aeronautics, mathematics, thermodynamics, astrogation, etc.

Planetary Science: This specialization is the study of environmental and planetary forces, such as hydrology, oceanography, mineralogy, geology, meteorology, climatology, etc. It is the preferred tool of the planetary surveyor.

item_description.p: Description: This is basically a hand-held video recorder, able to take video and audio recordings in a narrow or wide-field mode. When used passively, it simply records all that is seen or heard. When used actively by draining 2 EU from the attached energy source, it takes an extraordinarily detailed holographic full-range snapshot/image, which it can store and send to a computer for more detailed 3-dimensional analysis.

id-00002.active: cuts time in half using Technician skill

item_description.p: Description: The only passive use this hand-held device has is as a sophisticated technical tablet, with updated tech journals and published study results, maintenance schedules, disassembly schematics, and various other technical texts helpful to an engineer. You can wave it over a known piece of standard tech and get details on the basics: how to access its circuits, what screws to remove, etc. When used actively (drains 2 EU), it can perform a very comprehensive diagnostic scan of a subject. The results help by cutting in half the time it takes to perform a diagnosis, service, modification, or repair of a device or structure. A tech scanner is commonly carried by technicians on salvage missions.

id-00002.name: Tech Scanner

id-00003.active: “see” up to 3 m depth on screen

item_description.p: Description: This hand-held x-ray machine can passively detect the presence of x-rays, but otherwise won’t do too much. When activated (drains 2 EU), however, it shows a picture on a small screen of an x-ray picture of up to 3 m depth. The x-rays can be harmful to biological beings if exposed for a prolonged time, but are mostly harmless in such a short burst. Although it won’t see through very dense metals, such as lead, it’s a great tool for prospectors looking for veins of valuables in mountains or by burglars and technicians searching within walls for power lines or security camera feeds.

id-00003.name: X-Ray Scanner
id-00003.passive: detect presence of x-rays, alert user

item_description.p: Connections: 1

Description: These are roughly the size of an old AA-sized battery and are interchangeable among all energy-draining devices, such as weapons and defenses. They provide 20 EU (“energy units”) of usable energy. EU devices are not rechargeable and are discarded when depleted.
Training: Technician

Description: Techkits exist to abstract the many things that a capable technician must carry around to have a chance to repair so many types of technology. A techkit has tools, wires, diagnostic utilities, micro welder, laser cutters, calibrated measurement devices, common replacement circuits, jumpers, fuses, disposable one-shot anti- virus and troubleshooting software bundles, and much, much more. Techkits have 20 points of provisions. Each time a character repairs or jury rigs damage, it depletes these provision points. Repairs drain 1 provision point for every 5 BP (or fraction thereof) repaired. Other uses of a techkit (diagnostics, interfacing, programming robots or computers, restoring a damaged subsystem, etc.) drain 1. When the techkit is out of provisions, the technician is unable to repair equipment effectively. See Chapter 3: Skills for more information about Technicians and Techkits.

notes.p: A chatty, multi-tool robot with a knack for jury-rigging repairs and cracking security panels.

list.li: No security system. Exposed activation/deactivation switch and interface port.
Slow and methodical movement. Reduce listed MOVE rate by 1D (minimum 3) and cut Travel speed in half. Robot wasn’t designed for speed.

notes.p: General maintenance and repairs
Standard
Travel: 2kph

Throughout the galaxy in factories, hospitals, schools, and starships, you’ll find these hovering balls of multi-armed technology busy at work. They’re either pushing brooms and mops or running diagnostics or maintenance on environment systems, machinery, reactors, computers, structures, etc. They zoom around a modern city maintaining electrical systems and structural systems.

Maintenance bots are programmed to be all the skilled maintenance a facility or region needs to keep things running, though they sometimes need direction from a skilled sapient being to manage them efficiently. They’ve been known to waste time fixing a crack in a corridor while a Nova Drive leaks coolant all over a ship’s engineering deck.
No job is too big nor too menial for a maintenance bot!

description.p: This skill represents the analysis and classification of various facets of the universe and includes a very broad array of various types of sciences. All fields of science represent application of the scientific method to break down and understand the relationships of things in the frontier.
When you select this skill, you must specialize in one facet of scientific expertise , such as those shown below (or some other academic field permitted by the Referee).

list.li: Life Science: This specialization is the study of how biological subjects function and interact with one another and their environments. It represents such fields of science as botany, zoology, biology, psychology, xenobiology, ecology, etc.

Physical Science : This specialization is the study of the physical universe and how particles and chemistries exist and interact with one another. It represents such fields of science as physics, chemistry, photonics, aeronautics, mathematics, thermodynamics, astrogation, etc.

Planetary Science: This specialization is the study of environmental and planetary forces, such as hydrology, oceanography, mineralogy, geology, meteorology, climatology, etc. It is the preferred tool of the planetary surveyor.

description.p: This skill is added to ability checks relating to repairing things, engineering with electronic, mechanical, robotic, and computer systems. It is the skill of the mechanic, engineer, programmer, roboticist, or hacker. Most of what you can do with this skill requires a techkit, which has a limited number of provision points for working your trade. You’ll have to make sure you keep your techkit provisioned for adventures.
When you select this skill, you must specialize in one facet of technical expertise chosen from the list below (or some other technological field permitted by the Referee).

list.li: Computers : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of all types of computers and computerized hand-held equipment in the frontier.
Robots : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of all types of robots and robotic industrial equipment.

Starships : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of all types of space-faring vessels and vehicles (from shuttles and workpods to cruisers, and everything in between).
Structures : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of buildings, bridges, walls, bunkers, and other structural objects.
Vehicles : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of all types of vehicles (land, sea, hover, air, etc.).

item_description.p: Description: This is basically a hand-held video recorder, able to take video and audio recordings in a narrow or wide-field mode. When used passively, it simply records all that is seen or heard. When used actively by draining 2 EU from the attached energy source, it takes an extraordinarily detailed holographic full-range snapshot/image, which it can store and send to a computer for more detailed 3-dimensional analysis.

Description: When used passively, this hand-held device functions like a communicator. But when used actively (drains 2 EU), it performs a comprehensive scan of all wavelengths, looking for various types of amplitude, frequency, and phase modulations which might identify communication channels. It can actively demodulate these (unless they’re encoded) and produce audio for all to hear. If it detects video in the signal, it can show the video as well. As part of the active scan, it will indicate the direction of the signal’s source, and attempt to calculate the distance to its point of origin.

id-00003.active: cuts time in half using Technician skill

item_description.p: Description: The only passive use this hand-held device has is as a sophisticated technical tablet, with updated tech journals and published study results, maintenance schedules, disassembly schematics, and various other technical texts helpful to an engineer. You can wave it over a known piece of standard tech and get details on the basics: how to access its circuits, what screws to remove, etc. When used actively (drains 2 EU), it can perform a very comprehensive diagnostic scan of a subject. The results help by cutting in half the time it takes to perform a diagnosis, service, modification, or repair of a device or structure. A tech scanner is commonly carried by technicians on salvage missions.

id-00003.name: Tech Scanner

item_description.p: Connections: 2

Description: These are approximately the size of a fat wallet or small belt pouch. They possess two retractable cables ending in connectors the size of a power pack, allowing them to power two different devices simultaneously. They provide 50 EU of usable energy. EU devices are not rechargeable and are discarded when depleted.
Training: Technician

Description: Techkits exist to abstract the many things that a capable technician must carry around to have a chance to repair so many types of technology. A techkit has tools, wires, diagnostic utilities, micro welder, laser cutters, calibrated measurement devices, common replacement circuits, jumpers, fuses, disposable one-shot anti- virus and troubleshooting software bundles, and much, much more. Techkits have 20 points of provisions. Each time a character repairs or jury rigs damage, it depletes these provision points. Repairs drain 1 provision point for every 5 BP (or fraction thereof) repaired. Other uses of a techkit (diagnostics, interfacing, programming robots or computers, restoring a damaged subsystem, etc.) drain 1. When the techkit is out of provisions, the technician is unable to repair equipment effectively. See Chapter 3: Skills for more information about Technicians and Techkits.

notes.p: The ultimate DIY astronaut assistant, cobbled together from space junk and obsolete tech.

list.li: Extended diagnostic and charging requirements. Double down-time required (8 hours per day).
Poor self-repair circuitry. Robot only self-repairs at a rate of 1 BP per 2 days.

notes.p: Astrogation, maintenance, piloting
Standard
Travel: 75 kph

Astrogation is a complicated affair involving computations best left to scientists. To avoid paying a hefty wage to an astrogator, many captains purchase astro-tech bots. They are experts at piloting, handy at repairing damaged systems, and need no food or cabin space.

Astro-tech bots have ports from which up to four arms can emerge in order to control complex systems, and have telescopic head units to adjust visibility depending on the vehicle or cockpit shape. When not in some kind of cockpit, astro- tech bots roll around on wheels. They have trouble navigating stairs or other difficult terrain.

Astro-tech bots are commercially available all over the frontier, but buyer beware: it is suspected that an interstellar criminal organization has been programming them to lure unsuspecting crews to ambush points!

description.p: This skill is added to ability checks relating to controlling vehicles in stressful situations, including ground vehicles, aircraft, watercraft, and spaceships (though starships are piloted at -20 due to their complexity). Without an appropriate skill benefit, this skill cannot be used for piloting capital ships. Sample specializations include hover cycles, ground cars, aircars, jetcopters, etc. Adventurous characters will find themselves in vehicle chases now and then. Having someone with good piloting skills can significantly increase the character’s chances of surviving such high-speed situations.

Don’t think that training is required if all you’re wanting to do is fly or drive from point A to point B. No skill check will be required in order to pilot a vehicle in a non-stressful situation. Even a starship can be piloted without the requisite skill. But once weapons are brought to bear or a complex maneuver is required, you’re going to want a skilled pilot in the cockpit.

This skill represents the analysis and classification of various facets of the universe and includes a very broad array of various types of sciences. All fields of science represent application of the scientific method to break down and understand the relationships of things in the frontier.
When you select this skill, you must specialize in one facet of scientific expertise , such as those shown below (or some other academic field permitted by the Referee).

list.li: Life Science: This specialization is the study of how biological subjects function and interact with one another and their environments. It represents such fields of science as botany, zoology, biology, psychology, xenobiology, ecology, etc.

Physical Science : This specialization is the study of the physical universe and how particles and chemistries exist and interact with one another. It represents such fields of science as physics, chemistry, photonics, aeronautics, mathematics, thermodynamics, astrogation, etc.

Planetary Science: This specialization is the study of environmental and planetary forces, such as hydrology, oceanography, mineralogy, geology, meteorology, climatology, etc. It is the preferred tool of the planetary surveyor.

description.p: This skill is added to ability checks relating to repairing things, engineering with electronic, mechanical, robotic, and computer systems. It is the skill of the mechanic, engineer, programmer, roboticist, or hacker. Most of what you can do with this skill requires a techkit, which has a limited number of provision points for working your trade. You’ll have to make sure you keep your techkit provisioned for adventures.
When you select this skill, you must specialize in one facet of technical expertise chosen from the list below (or some other technological field permitted by the Referee).

list.li: Computers : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of all types of computers and computerized hand-held equipment in the frontier.
Robots : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of all types of robots and robotic industrial equipment.

Starships : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of all types of space-faring vessels and vehicles (from shuttles and workpods to cruisers, and everything in between).
Structures : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of buildings, bridges, walls, bunkers, and other structural objects.
Vehicles : This specialization refers to the mechanics, electronics, and software of all types of vehicles (land, sea, hover, air, etc.).

text.p: “Officially licensed by the Interstellar Outfitters Consortium, The Frontier Gear Guide showcases the latest in weapons, vehicles, and tactical tech from trusted brands across the Frontier. Accept no substitutes.”

The items in this chapter are detailed with a Rarity. This is a guideline to help Referees determine how common and availble these items are. It will help provide a structure for making these available to players as they gain loyalty benefits, if that's the way you wish to use them!
Civilian: items that are commonly availalbe in most all lcoations and generally unregulated.
Security: common items that may be restricted in some jurisdictions or may have somewhat limited availability.
Military: items that are usually restricted to posses and purchase, but are common among the various military and paramilitary organizations in the Frontier.
Cutting Edge: items that have limited availalbility within military or specific corporations. These might be proprietary technologies or even considered classified.

Founded and headquartered on Karys Tau, they also have a substantial faciltiies on Mir and Hardt Station. They focus on superior quality products and cutting-edge technology. They focus on military and large mercenary groups as their central clients. Only used versions of their products sold at retail outlets.
The unofficial company motto, touted by those fortunate enough to own their products, is "Expensive, but worth it."
All Apex firearms are designed with rail systems to allow for numerous weapon attachments so that users can customize and enhance them for their specific needs.
There is no doubt who the Grey Series of firearms is designed for. These firearms are highly sought by bodyguards and covert operatives alike.

list.li: Laser Target Red Dot, advantage on rolls to hit within 10m
Compact, +20% to CRD check to draw as one action

text.p: Rarity: Security
The White Series is designed for longer range battlefields with superior barrel quality and sights. Marketed for wilderness and regional operations.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges

text.p: Rarity: Civilian
Their Black series are their pinnacle series. These bring all their technolgies together; longer range, increased damage, and premium sights. At premium prices.

list.li: Calibrated Sights, +5 bonus to hit at all ranges

text.p: Rarity: Military
Designed to be a straightforward but high powered version of standard firearms. Marketed for Close Quarters Battle and urban engagements.
Rarity: Civilian

The following manufacturer's are some of the more prominent defense manufacturer's, or their products, that may show up in your campaign. The listed products are not all inclusive of their product's but rather a sampling to be used as needed.

One of the largest firearms manufacturers, with numerous locations including; Trudor, Mir, Perasia Prime, and Amon City. They make all standard firearms as well as cost effective variants, focusing on volume and market penetration.
Their unofficial motto, "Nothing special here, but just what you need!" Their official motto, "Equipping the Frontier for 600 years!"
This is their budget friendly no frills line. They work just fine, but if you are putting your life on the line, probably not what you want, but what you will find almost everywhere.

list.li: Cheap Construction, if fired more than once per turn, any failed roll to hit jams the weapon.

text.p: Rarity: Civilian
This is their "quality" line of products, and is represented with all the standard weapons from the Player's Handbook. Nothing better, nothing worse.
Rarity: Civilian

The only major arms manufacturer in the Kai Empire, they are located on Thritka. They are known for their cutting-edge silent sonic firearms. Given the nature of their technology, many governemnts are openly resistant to their sales and possesion, while their own clandestine groups are some of the companies most ardent buyers.
Using cutting edge technology, these sonic weapons are silent and just as dangerous as their competitors.

list.li: Silent. Weapon makes virtually nonoise when it fires

text.p: Rarity: Security - Military (locale dependent)
This line of sonic weapons have an improve lithium system that reduces power consumption.

list.li: Silent. Weapon makes virtually nonoise when it fires
Improved Power Consumption, power drain reduced by 20%

text.p: Rarity: Security - Military (locale dependent)

Located on Trudor, this company specializes in defensive suits, weapons and automated defense systems like security turrets. Besides standard versions of civilian, security and military defensive suits and screens, Stalwart has three unique product lines.
“Stalwart: Because retreat isn’t an option.”
A line of elite combat-rated suits for special forces. Not much more than prototypes in field trials by select unit of the Federation Task Force.

list.li: Ablative Ballistic Plates (ABP), +20 BP protection, not repairable

text.p: This line of defensive suits are favored by covert operatives. Though hard to acquire, these are the product that put Stalwart on the defense industry map.

list.li: +20 to resistance checks Stun (E) and Stun(S) checks

text.p: Infrared Motion Activated Automated Security Turrets. These are usually connected to a facility security system and computer, but can operatin in standalone mode.
IMAAST (NPCs):
<Company Overview>
<product line overview/traits>
<Product Details>
Headquartered in Lumbra, this company was the first motor works founded after the Novim arrival 7 centuries ago.

Specialtizing in hover vehicles, especially heavy-duty transports and skiffs. GravHawk’s TrailRaptor series dominates the badlands—hover-capable, armor-skinned, and built to tow half a colony’s worth of gear. Their vehicles are favored by survey crews, nomadic traders, and bounty teams who need reliability over style.

list.li: Improved Safety Systems, occupants take half damage

text.p: Headquartered Marxaxus with a satellite manufacturing facility on Invictus. They design and build jetcopters and VTOL patrol craft for security and military organizations.

Vigilant Class aircraft are the backbone of frontier airspace control. With whisper-quiet engines and omni-directional thrusters, they’re used by corporate security forces and mercenary companies alike.

list.li: Very quiet engine, cannot be heard easily

text.p: One of the largest terrestrial vehicle manufacturer’s in the Frontier. They have numerous facilities spread across the systems but are headquartered on Amon City. They produce all the standard vehicles, but are known for their Type R line of personal performance vehicles.

Type R vehicles are a cult favorite among couriers, racers, and smugglers. Lightweight, fast, and dangerously customizable, it’s the go-to for anyone who wants to thread the needle between canyon walls or zip through urban ruins.

list.li: Power Hungry, +1 additional parabattery or 1 Type higher

text.p: Specializing in high-performance watercraft, luxury performance cars, they are one of the premier exports from Tendarus.
The original product line from Mirek, it is still sought after as a performance luxury watercraft. Known for it's speed, handling and ability for cusotmization.

Falconet coupes are the envy of frontier elites—sleek, fast, and equipped with terrain-adaptive cruise modes. Though marketed as a luxury vehicle, its stealth systems and reinforced chassis make it a favorite of spies and rogue diplomats.

list.li: Improved Safety System, occupants take only half damage from any crashes or damage
Very quiet engine, cannot be heard easily
High-Tech Jamming Suite; IR, ECM, Lifeform & Sonic
High-Tech AI, adaptive cruise, +10 pilot checks

text.p: Located on Oberon, Holden Motors is the go-to manufacturer for personal off-road vehicles and ruggedized explorers designed for the harsh realities of Frontier life. Whether you're crossing irradiated salt flats, navigating fungal jungle terrain, or outrunning sandstorms on a terraformed moon, Holden rigs are built to endure.
These are boxy and cheap. They’re mass produced and common in the Frontier, even if they’re a bit uncomfortable, they endure. They’re one of the Hold’s largest exports.

Veltrax is the premier name in luxury personal vehicles across the outer colonies. While most frontier vehicles are built for survival, Veltrax crafts machines for those who demand comfort, style, and status—even in the dust-choked wilds. Their hover sedans glide like silk over cratered terrain, and their jetcycles are as much fashion statements as they are engineering marvels.

Veltrax vehicles are favored by corporate magnates, high-ranking diplomats, and charismatic rogue traders. Their designs blend cutting-edge tech with old-world elegance, often featuring rare materials, artisan finishes, and limited-edition variants. The company’s emblem—a stylized winged hourglass—symbolizes timeless luxury in motion.
A hover coupe with adaptive climate pods, privacy shielding, and AI-assisted terrain smoothing

list.li: Improved Safety System, occupants take only half damage from any crashes or damage
Bio-lock; only starts for programmed owner
Very quiet engine, cannot be heard easily

text.p: A twin-engine cyclejet with reactive paint, biometric ignition, and zero-lag steering

list.li: Bio-lock; only starts for programmed owner

text.p: A luxury off-road cruiser with retractable lounge canopy, gourmet food synthesizer, and panoramic holo-glass

list.li: Very quiet engine, cannot be heard easily

text.p: Karnex specializes in rugged, modular units designed for hazardous environments and frontier warfare. Their bots are infamous for their over-engineered durability and intimidating silhouettes. Specialties:
"Built for the Brutal"
A semi-autonomous bipedal unit with reactive armor plating and modular weapon hardpoints.

list.li: Armor, Military, quickly replaced armor plates, 1/2 total BP damage can be repaired in 1 hour
Improved security panel. Opening requires Technician check with -40 modifier unless passcode known.
Modular Hardpoints, integrated weapons can be swapped in 1 hour

text.p: A six-legged cargo hauler that can traverse unstable terrain and defend itself with shock-limb countermeasures.

list.li: Advantage with all AGL-based action checks. Designed to be flexible, balanced, and stable.

text.p: Vireo is the go-to for sleek, high-functioning robotics used in medical, diplomatic, and scientific roles. Their designs favor elegance, adaptability, and advanced AI heuristics.
“Precision. Grace. Intelligence.
A humanoid medical assistant capable of battlefield triage and surgical procedures.
A floating, voice-modulating liaison bot used in tense negotiations and covert diplomacy.

list.li: Universal linguist. Robot knows all languages and dialects known in the frontier at time of construction.
Improved security panel. Opening requires Technician check with -40 modifier unless passcode known.
Designer model. Worth 50% more value, mostly due to its name. Appearance and paint job is impressive, but that doesn’t make it any more effective at what it does.

text.p: Born in the scrapyards of the Outer Belt, Scraplight builds quirky, semi-legal bots from salvaged tech. Their products are unpredictable, affordable, and beloved by smugglers and tinkerers.
“If it sparks, we’ll sell it."
Though Scraplight doesn't really have standard models, here are some bot of typical characteristics.
A chatty, multi-tool robot with a knack for jury-rigging repairs and cracking security panels.

list.li: No security system. Exposed activation/deactivation switch and interface port.
Slow and methodical movement. Reduce listed MOVE rate by 1D (minimum 3) and cut Travel speed in half. Robot wasn’t designed for speed.

text.p: A swarm-capable surveillance bot that resembles a mechanical beetle and can self-destruct on command.

list.li: Exposed sensor cluster. If robot receives critical hit, it loses all scanners and is blinded, deafened, etc.
Disadvantage with all STR-based action checks. The robot’s structural system is light.

text.p: The ultimate DIY astronaut assistant, cobbled together from space junk and obsolete tech.

list.li: Extended diagnostic and charging requirements. Double down-time required (8 hours per day).
Poor self-repair circuitry. Robot only self-repairs at a rate of 1 BP per 2 days.

text.p: A cheerful assistant bot that thrives on organizing chaos and lending a mechanical hand.

list.li: Not water-safe. If submersed in water, circuitry fails and suffer from normal “drowning” rules.
Loud servo system. All of the robot’s movements are accompanied by a slight electrical noise.
Advantage with all STR-based action checks. Robot has extraordinary grip.
Disadvantage with all PER-based action checks. Poorly integrated scanner subsystem.

text.p: Welcome, gear-slings and tech-hustlers. You’ve just cracked open the ultimate lowdown on the tools that’ll keep your creds in your pocket and your enemies six feet under. I’m Captain Vorn, your reluctant tour guide through the galaxy’s most notorious arsenals, robot forges, motor works, and defense contractors. I’ve seen these rigs in action—sometimes they saved my hide; other times, not so much.

This isn’t corporate PR. It’s the unvarnished truth, with my curmudgeonly commentary baked in. You want specs? You’ll get ’em. You want jokes? I’ve got a few. You want warranties? Haha, good luck. Flip the page and steel yourself: the Frontier is merciless, and you’re going to need every advantage you can get.

list.li: 53 Unique Weapons & Accesories from Four Manufacturers
6 Unique Defensive Systems from One Manufacturer

text.p: All of these are guided by the "Specific <weapon/vehicle/robot> Models" sections of the FrontierSpace Referee's Guide and will help you add flavor and depth to the setting without you having to spend time doing it yourself. All of these are ready to run, setup and formatted for ease of use and ready reference.

Ready to dive deeper? Grab your favorite rail-system setup, oil your servo-joints, buckle your harness, and turn the page. The Frontier Gear Guide awaits—with enough firepower, firmware, and funky rides to keep you alive until the next heat sig hits your scanner.

id-00022.name: A Merc’s Companion to Firepower, Bots, and Wheels

text.p: If you’ve survived this far without getting vaporized or stranded in a sandstorm, congratulations—you’re about to get ahead of the curve. The Frontier Gear Guide is your hard-won cheat sheet to choosing the right weapons, bots, rides, and defenses before your next contract goes sideways.

Inside these pages you’ll find no-nonsense field wisdom, shoddy corporate slogans exposed for the lies they are, and honest ratings from someone who’s blown more holes in mercenary gear than I care to admit. Read it cover to cover, bookmark your favorites, and leave the rest for someone who likes surprises at thirty meters.
Consider this your personal survival kit. Trust it, or don’t—just don’t come crying when your cheap blaster jams and a Karnex Grudge Frame stomps your face in.
—Captain Vorn

Welcome, thrill-seekers and dust-choked drifters, to my no-holds-barred breakdown of every wheel, hover, and jet-prop contraption prowling the frontier. I’ve tasted sandstorms through radiator grills and watched half-baked prototypes implode in zero-G, so consider these my unsolicited battle reports. Strap in, prime your credit blasters, and get ready to judge rigs not by their paint job or price tag, but by how often they’ll haul you home in one piece.

These guys have been around forever, and then some, GravHawk’s TrailRaptors are the hulking hover haulers of the badlands. You want to tow half a colony’s worth of scrap through a sandstorm? TrailRaptor’s your beast. They’ll shrug off boulder impacts like mosquitoes—and they’ll carry your loot, crew, and possibly a small moon. Just don’t expect tight handling or fuel economy. These rigs are tanks with skiffs strapped to ’em.

Model 500s are the Frontier’s ubiquitous box-on-wheels: cheap, uncompromising, and as comfortable as a crate full of rocks. You’ll see them everywhere—from irradiated salt flats to fungal jungles—because they endure what lesser rigs would disintegrate in. Ride one if you’re packing minimal creds and maximum grit. Don’t ride one if you value lumbar support.

Type R vehicles are cult classics for a reason. They’re the caffeine shot of ground transport—light, absurdly fast, and begging you to customize with afterburners and razor-sharp spoilers. Perfect for couriers threading canyon needles or smugglers outrunning patrols. Downsides? Zero cargo space, high upkeep, and a tendency to remind you just how often you brake on a cliff’s edge.

WaveRunner water rockets: the original performance crafts, still the fastest way to shred a wake. If you’re skimming aquifer rivers or outracing rival gangs on lagoon runs, nothing beats Mirek hulls for speed and handling.

Falconet coupes: Frontier luxury turned spy asset. Sleek shoreline cruisers by day, stealth-capable GTs by night. They’ll out-glide a storm surge and slip past scanners with their terrain-adaptive cruise modes. Great for rogue diplomats who like their smuggling scenic.

Vigilant Class jetcopters are the unsung heroes of airspace control—or your personal flying nightmare. Whisper-quiet engines, omni thrusters that dance sideways, and enough armor to shrug off missile splinters. Corporate security loves ’em, mercs rent ’em, and you’ll hate to see one’s nav lights turning red in your six.

If survival’s your goal, skip Veltrax. If style, status, and zero-G comfort matter more, welcome to the pantheon. Seraph hover coupes cradle you in climate-controlled pods; LumaJets weave through traffic with biometrically keyed throttles; Pavilions roll across cratered plains with gourmet synth-food on tap. They cost more than a small moon colony—and they’re worth every credit… until someone with a rocket launcher decides you’re a target.

That’s the skinny on the heavy hitters, the speed demons, and the plush parade floats masquerading as survival tools. Pick your ride for the job, not the cantina photo op, and never forget: when your chassis cracks under pressure or your thrusters sputter out in the middle of nowhere, you’ll wish you’d heeded Captain Vorn’s grizzled counsel. Now get moving—somebody’s got to colonize those hellscapes.

p.i: Sstill cursing my last Veltrax LumaJet lease agreement

id-00025.name: Captain Vorn's Unsolicited Opinions on Vehicle Manufacturers

text.p: Look, I’m not here to tell you what to shoot—just what not to shoot with. You want my take on the big names in the bang-bang business? Fine. Just don’t blame me if you end up broke, deaf, or mysteriously vaporized.

Ah, Apex. The Karys Tau crown jewel. If you’ve got the creds and a taste for precision engineering that borders on fetishism, this is your jam. Their gear is sleek, modular, and so overbuilt it could probably survive reentry strapped to a meteor.
They don’t sell new units to civilians—only used ones trickle down to retail, usually with scorch marks and a few “custom” mods courtesy of some merc with a soldering iron and a death wish.
Unofficial motto? “Expensive, but worth it.”
Official motto? Probably something in Latin that translates to “We don’t care if you can’t afford it.”
Still, every Apex rifle comes with rail systems that let you slap on scopes, grips, launchers, espresso machines—whatever you need to feel tactical.
Now here’s the people’s gunmaker. You want a rifle that works? They’ve got it. You want a pistol that jams only occasionally? They’ve got that too.
Headquartered everywhere and nowhere—Trudor, Mir, Perasia Prime, Amon City—you name it, they’ve got a warehouse there.
Their motto is “Nothing special here, but just what you need!” which is corporate speak for “We make guns that shoot bullets, mostly.”
If Apex is a gourmet steakhouse, Frontier Armements is the galley slop that keeps you alive. Reliable, forgettable, and probably what your enemies are carrying too.

Ah, the fringe dweller’s favorite. Based out of Bora-grae, which is basically a rock with a flag, Lampyridae makes gyrojet weapons—those fancy little rocket bullets that whistle past your ear like a love letter from a lunatic.
They’ve got a reputation for ruggedness and freedom, which is code for “we sell to anyone with a pulse and a grudge.”

If you’re out past the beacon line and need something that works in zero-G, underwater, or inside a worm’s digestive tract, Lampyridae’s got your back. Just don’t expect customer service. Or instructions.
Now we’re getting weird. SST is the Kai Empire’s pride and paranoia. Their sonic weapons are silent, deadly, and probably illegal in half the systems you’ve visited.
Headquartered on Thritka, they specialize in firearms that don’t go bang—they go *thrum*. You won’t hear it, but your target will feel it. Briefly.
Governments hate them. Spooks love them. If you’re buying SST gear, you’re either a covert operative or about to become one by accident.
Just remember: if it doesn’t make noise, it doesn’t mean it’s not lethal. It just means you’ll be explaining it to customs for the next six hours.

So there you have it. Four companies, four flavors of mayhem. Choose wisely, shoot straight, and for the love of the Void, don’t mix Lampyridae rounds with Frontier's FieldLine gyros unless you enjoy spontaneous disassembly.

p.i: Currently armed, mildly annoyed, and always opinionated

id-00026.name: Captain Vorn’s Unsolicited Opinions on Firearms Manufacturers

text.p: Listen up, metal-mongers and circuit-slingers: I’ve had servo joints seize mid-sprint, watched hulking frames wrestle quakes into submission, and gasped as backyard tinkerers’ bots performed impromptu fireworks shows. Consider this your frontline dossier on the puppet masters you thought you could trust—before your next “helper” files a restraining order against you.

The good 'ol Grudge Combat droids, a semi-autonomous biped that wears reactive armor like a knight on espresso. Slap on weapons, point it at trouble, and watch it chew ballistic rounds for breakfast. Downsides? It’s about as subtle as a sonic boom in a library.

Then you got the Mulejack Heavies, six legs of pure grit, perfect for hauling mortared wreckage or that stash of illicit synth-spice across quake-riddled badlands. Toss it a cargo crate and it’ll trundle onward—until something actually tries to stop it, then prepare for a shock-limb surprise party.

Slick enough to star in holo-concerts, smart enough to diagnose your boss’s stress ulcer before you spill corporate secrets, and priced like it comes with its own moon. These bots glide through med-bays, negotiating tables, and quantum labs without so much as a whiff of panic. Just don’t ask one to haul rocks—its soft-touch grip might tear you up for even suggesting it.

Born where the junk piles grow taller than space elevators, these semi-legal misfits are a hacker’s dream and a clean-tech lobby’s worst nightmare. Consistency? Optional. Personality quirks? Guaranteed.

Clatterjacks are a companion droid you won't know if you want to worship or deep six. Chatty, armed with every jury-rigged tool you can think of, and annoyingly helpful at 3 a.m. when you’re cursing fused conduits. Warning: may unsolicitedly debug your love life while fixing your coolant leak.

The you got your Scuttlebugs, a beetle-like surveillance swarm with self-destruct payloads that go boom on command. Great for gathering intel… and making sure your enemies never collect that data breach.
Pick your tin god by the chaos you can tolerate and the flair you can afford. Miss the memo, and you’ll be begging for a warranty from a toaster. Over and out

p.i: Still oiling my Mulejack’s taste for jagged rocks

id-00027.name: Captain Vorn's Unsolicitied Opinions on Robot Manufacturers

text.p: The items in this chapter are detailed with a Rarity. This is a guideline to help Referees determine how common and availble these items are. It will help provide a structure for making these available to players as they gain loyalty benefits, if that's the way you wish to use them!
Civilian: items that are commonly availalbe in most all lcoations and generally unregulated.
Security: common items that may be restricted in some jurisdictions or may have somewhat limited availability.
Military: items that are usually restricted to posses and purchase, but are common among the various military and paramilitary organizations in the Frontier.
Cutting Edge: items that have limited availalbility within military or specific corporations. These might be proprietary technologies or even considered classified.

Headquartered on Bora-grae this releatively small company has a reputation for freedom and ruggedness, and supplying weapons to those on the fringes. They generally produce gyrojet weapons and ammunition.
Lampyridae gyrojets are rugged and are standard with extended magazines.

list.li: Rugged designs that are known to rarely misfire even in extreme circumstances, but they are bulky. -20% to CRD checks to quickdraw the weapon as a single action.
Add 50% to the number of shots in one payload. If weapon uses EU, reduce the EU drain per shot by 1 (minimum 1 EU/shot).

text.p: Rarity: Civilian

These rounds come in different types, each deals either Electric & Sonic (often outlawed) damage. They cost 15 cr per bullet and are only sold to a very limited cleintelle (production is very limited). Common usage is to mix types in a clip to insure the ability to overcome defenses.
Rarity: Cutting-Edge
Extended Range rounds +20m range, but only do 2D damage, cost 5Cr.
Rarity: Security
Armor Piercing rounds, i.e. penetrating rounds half damage to suits, and half to target. Screens protect fully. cost 8 Cr.
Rarity: Military
